先听总故事

FAQ

这一页像一本关于“FAQ”的小绘本，它先告诉我们这位机器人朋友会做什么，再一步一步解释什么时候该这样做。 你可以先把它想成：FAQ。

原始路径：/help/faq 章节数量：20 查看原文

第 1 站

Start Here

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Start Here”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“FAQ”。

原文小纸条

# FAQ

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Quick answers plus deeper troubleshooting for real-world setups (local dev, VPS, multi-机器人朋友, OAuth/对话接口 keys, 聪明脑袋模型 f…”。

原文小纸条

Quick answers plus deeper troubleshooting for real-world setups (local dev, VPS, multi-agent, OAuth/API keys, model failover). For runtime diagnostics, see Troubleshooting. For the full config reference, see Configuration.

第 2 站

First 60 seconds if something is broken

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“First 60 seconds if something is broken”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“1. **Quick status (first check)**”。

原文小纸条

1. **Quick status (first check)**

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw status ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Fast local summary: OS + update, 门口的小门卫/service reachability, 机器人朋友s/sessions, provider 设置说明书 + runtime issues (when 门口…”。

原文小纸条

Fast local summary: OS + update, gateway/service reachability, agents/sessions, provider config + runtime issues (when gateway is reachable).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“2. **Pasteable report (safe to share)**”。

原文小纸条

2. **Pasteable report (safe to share)**

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw status --all ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Read-only diagnosis with log tail (tokens redacted).”。

原文小纸条

Read-only diagnosis with log tail (tokens redacted).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“3. **Daemon + port state**”。

原文小纸条

3. **Daemon + port state**

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw gateway status ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Shows supervisor runtime vs RPC reachability, the probe target URL, and which 设置说明书 the service likely used.”。

原文小纸条

Shows supervisor runtime vs RPC reachability, the probe target URL, and which config the service likely used.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“4. **Deep probes**”。

原文小纸条

4. **Deep probes**

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw status --deep ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Runs 门口的小门卫 health checks + provider probes (requires a reachable 门口的小门卫). See Health.”。

原文小纸条

Runs gateway health checks + provider probes (requires a reachable gateway). See Health.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“5. **Tail the latest log**”。

原文小纸条

5. **Tail the latest log**

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw logs --follow ``

像讲绘本

第 3 站

Quick start and first-run setup

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Quick start and first-run setup”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="I am stuck, fastest way to get unstuck"> Use a local AI 机器人朋友 that can **see your…”。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="I am stuck, fastest way to get unstuck"> Use a local AI agent that can **see your machine**. That is far more effective than asking in Discord, because most "I'm stuck" cases are **local config or environment issues** that remote helpers cannot inspect.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Claude Code**: https://www.anthropic.co…、**OpenAI Codex**: https://openai.com/code…。

原文小纸条
  • **Claude Code**: https://www.anthropic.com/claude-code/
  • **OpenAI Codex**: https://openai.com/codex/
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“These 工具小帮手s can read the repo, run 魔法口令s, inspect logs, and help fix your machine-level setup (PATH, services, permiss…”。

原文小纸条

These tools can read the repo, run commands, inspect logs, and help fix your machine-level setup (PATH, services, permissions, auth files). Give them the **full source checkout** via the hackable (git) install:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash -s -- --install-method git ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“This installs OpenClaw **from a git checkout**, so the 机器人朋友 can read the code + docs and reason about the exact versio…”。

原文小纸条

This installs OpenClaw **from a git checkout**, so the agent can read the code + docs and reason about the exact version you are running. You can always switch back to stable later by re-running the installer without --install-method git.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Tip: ask the 机器人朋友 to **plan and supervise** the fix (step-by-step), then execute only the necessary 魔法口令s. That keeps…”。

原文小纸条

Tip: ask the agent to **plan and supervise** the fix (step-by-step), then execute only the necessary commands. That keeps changes small and easier to audit.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If you discover a real bug or fix, please file a GitHub issue or send a PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues…”。

原文小纸条

If you discover a real bug or fix, please file a GitHub issue or send a PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pulls

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Start with these 魔法口令s (share outputs when asking for help):”。

原文小纸条

Start with these commands (share outputs when asking for help):

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw status openclaw models status openclaw doctor ``

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：openclaw status: quick snapshot of 门口的小门卫…、openclaw 聪明脑袋模型s status: checks provider…、openclaw doctor: validates and repairs co…。

原文小纸条
  • openclaw status: quick snapshot of gateway/agent health + basic config.
  • openclaw models status: checks provider auth + model availability.
  • openclaw doctor: validates and repairs common config/state issues.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Other useful CLI checks: openclaw status --all, openclaw logs --follow, openclaw 门口的小门卫 status, openclaw health --verbo…”。

原文小纸条

Other useful CLI checks: openclaw status --all, openclaw logs --follow, openclaw gateway status, openclaw health --verbose.

Quick debug loop: First 60 seconds if something is broken. Install docs: Install, Installer flags, Updating. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Recommended way to install and set up OpenClaw"> The repo recommends running from source and using on…”。

原文小纸条

If you don't have a global install yet, run it via pnpm openclaw onboard. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do I open the dashboard after onboarding?"> The wizard opens your browser with a clean (non-token…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I open the dashboard after onboarding?"> The wizard opens your browser with a clean (non-tokenized) dashboard URL right after onboarding and also prints the link in the summary. Keep that tab open; if it didn't launch, copy/paste the printed URL on the same machine. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I authenticate the dashboard (token) on localhost vs remote?"> **Localhost (same machine):**

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Open http://127.0.0.1:18789/.、If it asks for auth, paste the token from…、Retrieve it from the 门口的小门卫 host: opencla…。

原文小纸条
  • Open http://127.0.0.1:18789/.
  • If it asks for auth, paste the token from gateway.auth.token (or OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN) into Control UI settings.
  • Retrieve it from the gateway host: openclaw config get gateway.auth.token (or generate one: openclaw doctor --generate-gateway-token).
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“**Not on localhost:**”。

原文小纸条

**Not on localhost:**

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Tailscale Serve** (recommended): keep b…、**Tailnet bind**: run openclaw 门口的小门卫 --b…、**SSH tunnel**: ssh -N -L 18789:127.0.0.1…。

原文小纸条
  • **Tailscale Serve** (recommended): keep bind loopback, run openclaw gateway --tailscale serve, open https://<magicdns>/. If gateway.auth.allowTailscale is true, identity headers satisfy Control UI/WebSocket auth (no token, assumes trusted gateway host); HTTP APIs still require token/password.
  • **Tailnet bind**: run openclaw gateway --bind tailnet --token "<token>", open http://<tailscale-ip>:18789/, paste token in dashboard settings.
  • **SSH tunnel**: ssh -N -L 18789:127.0.0.1:18789 user@host then open http://127.0.0.1:18789/ and paste the token in Control UI settings.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“See Dashboard and Web surfaces for bind modes and auth details. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

See Dashboard and Web surfaces for bind modes and auth details. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="What runtime do I need?"> Node **>= 22** is required. pnpm is recommended. Bun is **not recommended**…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="What runtime do I need?"> Node **>= 22** is required. pnpm is recommended. Bun is **not recommended** for the Gateway. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Does it run on Raspberry Pi?"> Yes. The 门口的小门卫 is lightweight - docs list **512MB-1GB RAM**, **1 core…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Does it run on Raspberry Pi?"> Yes. The Gateway is lightweight - docs list **512MB-1GB RAM**, **1 core**, and about **500MB** disk as enough for personal use, and note that a **Raspberry Pi 4 can run it**.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If you want extra headroom (logs, media, other services), **2GB is recommended**, but it's not a hard minimum.”。

原文小纸条

If you want extra headroom (logs, media, other services), **2GB is recommended**, but it's not a hard minimum.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Tip: a small Pi/VPS can host the 门口的小门卫, and you can pair **nodes** on your laptop/phone for local screen/camera/canvas…”。

原文小纸条

Tip: a small Pi/VPS can host the Gateway, and you can pair **nodes** on your laptop/phone for local screen/camera/canvas or command execution. See Nodes. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Any tips for Raspberry Pi installs?"> Short version: it works, but expect rough edges.”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Any tips for Raspberry Pi installs?"> Short version: it works, but expect rough edges.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Use a **64-bit** OS and keep Node >= 22.、Prefer the **hackable (git) install** so…、Start without 消息通道s/skills, then add them…、If you hit weird binary issues, it is usu…。

原文小纸条
  • Use a **64-bit** OS and keep Node >= 22.
  • Prefer the **hackable (git) install** so you can see logs and update fast.
  • Start without channels/skills, then add them one by one.
  • If you hit weird binary issues, it is usually an **ARM compatibility** problem.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Linux, Install. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Linux, Install. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="It is stuck on wake up my friend / onboarding will not hatch. What now?"> That screen depends on the…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="It is stuck on wake up my friend / onboarding will not hatch. What now?"> That screen depends on the Gateway being reachable and authenticated. The TUI also sends "Wake up, my friend!" automatically on first hatch. If you see that line with **no reply** and tokens stay at 0, the agent never ran.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“1. Restart the 门口的小门卫:”。

原文小纸条

1. Restart the Gateway:

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw gateway restart ``

原文小纸条

2. Check status + auth:

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“3. If it still hangs, run:”。

原文小纸条

3. If it still hangs, run:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw doctor ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If the 门口的小门卫 is remote, ensure the tunnel/Tailscale connection is up and that the UI is pointed at the right 门口的小门卫. S…”。

原文小纸条

If the Gateway is remote, ensure the tunnel/Tailscale connection is up and that the UI is pointed at the right Gateway. See Remote access. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can I migrate my setup to a new machine (Mac mini) without redoing onboarding?"> Yes. Copy the **stat…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I migrate my setup to a new machine (Mac mini) without redoing onboarding?"> Yes. Copy the **state directory** and **workspace**, then run Doctor once. This keeps your bot "exactly the same" (memory, session history, auth, and channel state) as long as you copy **both** locations:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“1. Install OpenClaw on the new machine. 2. Copy $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR (default: ~/.openclaw) from the old machine. 3. Cop…”。

原文小纸条

1. Install OpenClaw on the new machine. 2. Copy $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR (default: ~/.openclaw) from the old machine. 3. Copy your workspace (default: ~/.openclaw/workspace). 4. Run openclaw doctor and restart the Gateway service.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“That preserves 设置说明书, auth profiles, WhatsApp creds, sessions, and memory. If you're in remote mode, remember the 门口的小门…”。

原文小纸条

That preserves config, auth profiles, WhatsApp creds, sessions, and memory. If you're in remote mode, remember the gateway host owns the session store and workspace.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“**Important:** if you only commit/push your workspace to GitHub, you're backing up **memory + bootstrap files**, but **…”。

原文小纸条

**Important:** if you only commit/push your workspace to GitHub, you're backing up **memory + bootstrap files**, but **not** session history or auth. Those live under ~/.openclaw/ (for example ~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/sessions/).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Related: Migrating, Where things live on disk, 机器人朋友 workspace, Doctor, Remote mode. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Related: Migrating, Where things live on disk, Agent workspace, Doctor, Remote mode. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Where do I see what is new in the latest version?"> Check the GitHub changelog: https://github.com/op…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Where do I see what is new in the latest version?"> Check the GitHub changelog: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Newest entries are at the top. If the top section is marked **Unreleased**, the next dated section is the latest shippe…”。

原文小纸条

Newest entries are at the top. If the top section is marked **Unreleased**, the next dated section is the latest shipped version. Entries are grouped by **Highlights**, **Changes**, and **Fixes** (plus docs/other sections when needed). </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Cannot access docs.openclaw.ai (SSL error)"> Some Comcast/Xfinity connections incorrectly block docs.…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Cannot access docs.openclaw.ai (SSL error)"> Some Comcast/Xfinity connections incorrectly block docs.openclaw.ai via Xfinity Advanced Security. Disable it or allowlist docs.openclaw.ai, then retry. Please help us unblock it by reporting here: https://spa.xfinity.com/check\_url\_status.

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If you still can't reach the site, the docs are mirrored on GitHub: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/tree/main/docs…”。

原文小纸条

If you still can't reach the site, the docs are mirrored on GitHub: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/tree/main/docs </Accordion>

<Accordion title="Difference between stable and beta"> **Stable** and **beta** are **npm dist-tags**, not separate code lines:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：latest = stable、beta = early build for testing。

原文小纸条
  • latest = stable
  • beta = early build for testing
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“We ship builds to **beta**, test them, and once a build is solid we **promote that same version to latest**. That's why…”。

原文小纸条

We ship builds to **beta**, test them, and once a build is solid we **promote that same version to latest**. That's why beta and stable can point at the **same version**.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“See what changed: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md </Accordion>”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do I install the beta version and what is the difference between beta and dev?"> **Beta** is the…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I install the beta version and what is the difference between beta and dev?"> **Beta** is the npm dist-tag beta (may match latest). **Dev** is the moving head of main (git); when published, it uses the npm dist-tag dev.

原文小纸条

  • Fly.io
  • Hetzner
  • exe.dev
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“How it works in the cloud: the **门口的小门卫 runs on the 大房子服务器**, and you access it from your laptop/phone via the Control…”。

原文小纸条

How it works in the cloud: the **Gateway runs on the server**, and you access it from your laptop/phone via the Control UI (or Tailscale/SSH). Your state + workspace live on the server, so treat the host as the source of truth and back it up.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“You can pair **nodes** (Mac/iOS/Android/headless) to that cloud 门口的小门卫 to access local screen/camera/canvas or run 魔法口令…”。

原文小纸条

You can pair **nodes** (Mac/iOS/Android/headless) to that cloud Gateway to access local screen/camera/canvas or run commands on your laptop while keeping the Gateway in the cloud.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Hub: Platforms. Remote access: 门口的小门卫 remote. Nodes: Nodes, Nodes CLI. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Hub: Platforms. Remote access: Gateway remote. Nodes: Nodes, Nodes CLI. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can I ask OpenClaw to update itself?"> Short answer: **possible, not recommended**. The update flow c…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I ask OpenClaw to update itself?"> Short answer: **possible, not recommended**. The update flow can restart the Gateway (which drops the active session), may need a clean git checkout, and can prompt for confirmation. Safer: run updates from a shell as the operator.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Use the CLI:”。

原文小纸条

Use the CLI:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw update openclaw update status openclaw update --channel stable|beta|dev openclaw update --tag <dist-tag|version> openclaw update --no-restart ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If you must automate from an 机器人朋友:”。

原文小纸条

If you must automate from an agent:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw update --yes --no-restart openclaw gateway restart ``

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Update, Updating. </Accordion>”。

Docs: Update, Updating. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="What does onboarding actually do?"> openclaw onboard is the recommended setup path. In **local mode**…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="What does onboarding actually do?"> openclaw onboard is the recommended setup path. In **local mode** it walks you through:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**聪明脑袋模型/auth setup** (provider OAuth/set…、**Workspace** location + bootstrap files、**门口的小门卫 settings** (bind/port/auth/tails…、**Providers** (WhatsApp, Telegram, Discor…。

原文小纸条
  • **Model/auth setup** (provider OAuth/setup-token flows and API keys supported, plus local model options such as LM Studio)
  • **Workspace** location + bootstrap files
  • **Gateway settings** (bind/port/auth/tailscale)
  • **Providers** (WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, Mattermost (plugin), Signal, iMessage)
  • **Daemon install** (LaunchAgent on macOS; systemd user unit on Linux/WSL2)
  • **Health checks** and **skills** selection
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“It also warns if your 设置说明书ured 聪明脑袋模型 is unknown or missing auth. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

It also warns if your configured model is unknown or missing auth. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Do I need a Claude or OpenAI subscription to run this?"> No. You can run OpenClaw with **对话接口 keys**…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Do I need a Claude or OpenAI subscription to run this?"> No. You can run OpenClaw with **API keys** (Anthropic/OpenAI/others) or with **local-only models** so your data stays on your device. Subscriptions (Claude Pro/Max or OpenAI Codex) are optional ways to authenticate those providers.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If you choose Anthropic subscription auth, decide for yourself whether to use it: Anthropic has blocked some subscripti…”。

原文小纸条

If you choose Anthropic subscription auth, decide for yourself whether to use it: Anthropic has blocked some subscription usage outside Claude Code in the past. OpenAI Codex OAuth is explicitly supported for external tools like OpenClaw.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Anthropic, OpenAI, Local 聪明脑袋模型s, 聪明脑袋模型s. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Anthropic, OpenAI, Local models, Models. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can I use Claude Max subscription without an 对话接口 key?"> Yes. You can either use a **setup-token** or…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I use Claude Max subscription without an API key?"> Yes. You can either use a **setup-token** or reuse a local **Claude CLI** login on the gateway host.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Claude Pro/Max subscriptions **do not include an 对话接口 key**, so this is the technical path for subscription accounts. B…”。

原文小纸条

Claude Pro/Max subscriptions **do not include an API key**, so this is the technical path for subscription accounts. But this is your decision: Anthropic has blocked some subscription usage outside Claude Code in the past. If you want the clearest and safest supported path for production, use an Anthropic API key. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How does Anthropic setup-token auth work?"> claude setup-token generates a **token string** via the C…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How does Anthropic setup-token auth work?"> claude setup-token generates a **token string** via the Claude Code CLI (it is not available in the web console). You can run it on **any machine**. Choose **Anthropic token (paste setup-token)** in onboarding or paste it with openclaw models auth paste-token --provider anthropic. The token is stored as an auth profile for the **anthropic** provider and used like an API key (no auto-refresh). More detail: OAuth. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Where do I find an Anthropic setup-token?"> It is **not** in the Anthropic Console. The setup-token i…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Where do I find an Anthropic setup-token?"> It is **not** in the Anthropic Console. The setup-token is generated by the **Claude Code CLI** on **any machine**:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} claude setup-token ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Copy the token it prints, then choose **Anthropic token (paste setup-token)** in onboarding. If you want to run it on t…”。

原文小纸条

Copy the token it prints, then choose **Anthropic token (paste setup-token)** in onboarding. If you want to run it on the gateway host, use openclaw models auth setup-token --provider anthropic. If you ran claude setup-token elsewhere, paste it on the gateway host with openclaw models auth paste-token --provider anthropic. See Anthropic. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Do you support Claude subscription auth (Claude Pro or Max)?"> Yes. You can either:”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Do you support Claude subscription auth (Claude Pro or Max)?"> Yes. You can either:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：use a **setup-token**、reuse a local **Claude CLI** login on the…。

原文小纸条
  • use a **setup-token**
  • reuse a local **Claude CLI** login on the gateway host with openclaw models auth login --provider anthropic --method cli --set-default
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Setup-token is still supported. Claude CLI migration is simpler when the 门口的小门卫 host already runs Claude Code. See Anth…”。

原文小纸条

Setup-token is still supported. Claude CLI migration is simpler when the gateway host already runs Claude Code. See Anthropic and OAuth.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Important: this is technical compatibility, not a policy guarantee. Anthropic has blocked some subscription usage outsi…”。

原文小纸条

Important: this is technical compatibility, not a policy guarantee. Anthropic has blocked some subscription usage outside Claude Code in the past. You need to decide whether to use it and verify Anthropic's current terms. For production or multi-user workloads, Anthropic API key auth is the safer, recommended choice. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Why am I seeing HTTP 429 rate_limit_error from Anthropic?"> That means your **Anthropic quota/rate li…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Why am I seeing HTTP 429 rate_limit_error from Anthropic?"> That means your **Anthropic quota/rate limit** is exhausted for the current window. If you use a **Claude subscription** (setup-token), wait for the window to reset or upgrade your plan. If you use an **Anthropic API key**, check the Anthropic Console for usage/billing and raise limits as needed.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If the 小纸条消息 is specifically: Extra usage is required for long context requests, the request is trying to use Anthropic…”。

原文小纸条

If the message is specifically: Extra usage is required for long context requests, the request is trying to use Anthropic's 1M context beta (context1m: true). That only works when your credential is eligible for long-context billing (API key billing or subscription with Extra Usage enabled).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Tip: set a **fallback 聪明脑袋模型** so OpenClaw can keep replying while a provider is rate-limited. See 聪明脑袋模型s, OAuth, and…”。

原文小纸条

Tip: set a **fallback model** so OpenClaw can keep replying while a provider is rate-limited. See Models, OAuth, and /gateway/troubleshooting#anthropic-429-extra-usage-required-for-long-context. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Is AWS Bedrock supported?"> Yes - via pi-ai's **Amazon Bedrock (Converse)** provider with **manual 设置…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Is AWS Bedrock supported?"> Yes - via pi-ai's **Amazon Bedrock (Converse)** provider with **manual config**. You must supply AWS credentials/region on the gateway host and add a Bedrock provider entry in your models config. See Amazon Bedrock and Model providers. If you prefer a managed key flow, an OpenAI-compatible proxy in front of Bedrock is still a valid option. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How does Codex auth work?"> OpenClaw supports **OpenAI Code (Codex)** via OAuth (ChatGPT sign-in). On…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How does Codex auth work?"> OpenClaw supports **OpenAI Code (Codex)** via OAuth (ChatGPT sign-in). Onboarding can run the OAuth flow and will set the default model to openai-codex/gpt-5.4 when appropriate. See Model providers and Onboarding (CLI). </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Do you support OpenAI subscription auth (Codex OAuth)?"> Yes. OpenClaw fully supports **OpenAI Code (…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Do you support OpenAI subscription auth (Codex OAuth)?"> Yes. OpenClaw fully supports **OpenAI Code (Codex) subscription OAuth**. OpenAI explicitly allows subscription OAuth usage in external tools/workflows like OpenClaw. Onboarding can run the OAuth flow for you.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“See OAuth, 聪明脑袋模型 providers, and Onboarding (CLI). </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

See OAuth, Model providers, and Onboarding (CLI). </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do I set up Gemini CLI OAuth?"> Gemini CLI uses a **新本领插件 auth flow**, not a 来帮忙的小伙伴 id or secret…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I set up Gemini CLI OAuth?"> Gemini CLI uses a **plugin auth flow**, not a client id or secret in openclaw.json.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Steps:”。

原文小纸条

Steps:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“1. Enable the 新本领插件: openclaw 新本领插件s enable google 2. Login: openclaw 聪明脑袋模型s auth login --provider google-gemini-cli -…”。

原文小纸条

1. Enable the plugin: openclaw plugins enable google 2. Login: openclaw models auth login --provider google-gemini-cli --set-default

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“This stores OAuth tokens in auth profiles on the 门口的小门卫 host. Details: 聪明脑袋模型 providers. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

This stores OAuth tokens in auth profiles on the gateway host. Details: Model providers. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Is a local 聪明脑袋模型 OK for casual chats?"> Usually no. OpenClaw needs large context + strong safety; sm…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Is a local model OK for casual chats?"> Usually no. OpenClaw needs large context + strong safety; small cards truncate and leak. If you must, run the **largest** MiniMax M2.5 build you can locally (LM Studio) and see /gateway/local-models. Smaller/quantized models increase prompt-injection risk - see Security. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do I keep hosted 聪明脑袋模型 traffic in a specific region?"> Pick region-pinned endpoints. OpenRouter…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I keep hosted model traffic in a specific region?"> Pick region-pinned endpoints. OpenRouter exposes US-hosted options for MiniMax, Kimi, and GLM; choose the US-hosted variant to keep data in-region. You can still list Anthropic/OpenAI alongside these by using models.mode: "merge" so fallbacks stay available while respecting the regioned provider you select. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Do I have to buy a Mac Mini to install this?"> No. OpenClaw runs on macOS or Linux (Windows via WSL2)…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Do I have to buy a Mac Mini to install this?"> No. OpenClaw runs on macOS or Linux (Windows via WSL2). A Mac mini is optional - some people buy one as an always-on host, but a small VPS, home server, or Raspberry Pi-class box works too.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“You only need a Mac **for macOS-only 工具小帮手s**. For i小纸条消息, use BlueBubbles (recommended) - the BlueBubbles 大房子服务器 runs…”。

原文小纸条

You only need a Mac **for macOS-only tools**. For iMessage, use BlueBubbles (recommended) - the BlueBubbles server runs on any Mac, and the Gateway can run on Linux or elsewhere. If you want other macOS-only tools, run the Gateway on a Mac or pair a macOS node.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: BlueBubbles, Nodes, Mac remote mode. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: BlueBubbles, Nodes, Mac remote mode. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Do I need a Mac mini for i小纸条消息 support?"> You need **some macOS device** signed into 小纸条消息s. It does…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Do I need a Mac mini for iMessage support?"> You need **some macOS device** signed into Messages. It does **not** have to be a Mac mini - any Mac works. **Use BlueBubbles** (recommended) for iMessage - the BlueBubbles server runs on macOS, while the Gateway can run on Linux or elsewhere.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Common setups:”。

原文小纸条

Common setups:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Run the 门口的小门卫 on Linux/VPS, and run the…、Run everything on the Mac if you want the…。

原文小纸条
  • Run the Gateway on Linux/VPS, and run the BlueBubbles server on any Mac signed into Messages.
  • Run everything on the Mac if you want the simplest single-machine setup.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: BlueBubbles, Nodes, Mac remote mode. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: BlueBubbles, Nodes, Mac remote mode. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="If I buy a Mac mini to run OpenClaw, can I connect it to my MacBook Pro?"> Yes. The **Mac mini can ru…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="If I buy a Mac mini to run OpenClaw, can I connect it to my MacBook Pro?"> Yes. The **Mac mini can run the Gateway**, and your MacBook Pro can connect as a **node** (companion device). Nodes don't run the Gateway - they provide extra capabilities like screen/camera/canvas and system.run on that device.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Common pattern:”。

原文小纸条

Common pattern:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：门口的小门卫 on the Mac mini (always-on).、MacBook Pro runs the macOS app or a node…、Use openclaw nodes status / openclaw node…。

原文小纸条
  • Gateway on the Mac mini (always-on).
  • MacBook Pro runs the macOS app or a node host and pairs to the Gateway.
  • Use openclaw nodes status / openclaw nodes list to see it.
像讲绘本

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I use Bun?"> Bun is **not recommended**. We see runtime bugs, especially with WhatsApp and Telegram. Use **Node** for stable gateways.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If you still want to experiment with Bun, do it on a non-production 门口的小门卫 without WhatsApp/Telegram. </Accordion>”。

If you still want to experiment with Bun, do it on a non-production gateway without WhatsApp/Telegram. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Telegram: what goes in allowFrom?"> 消息通道s.telegram.allowFrom is **the human sender's Telegram user ID…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Telegram: what goes in allowFrom?"> channels.telegram.allowFrom is **the human sender's Telegram user ID** (numeric). It is not the bot username.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Onboarding accepts @username input and resolves it to a numeric ID, but OpenClaw authorization uses numeric IDs only.”。

原文小纸条

Onboarding accepts @username input and resolves it to a numeric ID, but OpenClaw authorization uses numeric IDs only.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：DM @userinfobot or @getidsbot.

  • DM @userinfobot or @getidsbot.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“See /消息通道s/telegram. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

See /channels/telegram. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can multiple people use one WhatsApp number with different OpenClaw instances?"> Yes, via **multi-机器人…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can multiple people use one WhatsApp number with different OpenClaw instances?"> Yes, via **multi-agent routing**. Bind each sender's WhatsApp **DM** (peer kind: "direct", sender E.164 like +15551234567) to a different agentId, so each person gets their own workspace and session store. Replies still come from the **same WhatsApp account**, and DM access control (channels.whatsapp.dmPolicy / channels.whatsapp.allowFrom) is global per WhatsApp account. See Multi-Agent Routing and WhatsApp. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can I run a &#x22;fast chat&#x22; 机器人朋友 and an &#x22;Opus for coding&#x22; 机器人朋友?"> Yes. Use multi-机器…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I run a &#x22;fast chat&#x22; agent and an &#x22;Opus for coding&#x22; agent?"> Yes. Use multi-agent routing: give each agent its own default model, then bind inbound routes (provider account or specific peers) to each agent. Example config lives in Multi-Agent Routing. See also Models and Configuration. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Does Homebrew work on Linux?"> Yes. Homebrew supports Linux (Linuxbrew). Quick setup:”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Does Homebrew work on Linux?"> Yes. Homebrew supports Linux (Linuxbrew). Quick setup:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)" echo 'eval "$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv)"' >> ~/.profile eval "$(/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/brew shellenv)" brew install <formula> ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If you run OpenClaw via systemd, ensure the service PATH includes /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin (or your brew prefix)…”。

原文小纸条

If you run OpenClaw via systemd, ensure the service PATH includes /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin (or your brew prefix) so brew-installed tools resolve in non-login shells. Recent builds also prepend common user bin dirs on Linux systemd services (for example ~/.local/bin, ~/.npm-global/bin, ~/.local/share/pnpm, ~/.bun/bin) and honor PNPM_HOME, NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX, BUN_INSTALL, VOLTA_HOME, ASDF_DATA_DIR, NVM_DIR, and FNM_DIR when set. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Difference between the hackable git install and npm install">”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Difference between the hackable git install and npm install">

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“You run builds locally and can patch code/docs.”。

原文小纸条

You run builds locally and can patch code/docs.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Updates come from npm dist-tags.”。

原文小纸条

Updates come from npm dist-tags.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Hackable (git) install:** full source c…、**npm install:** global CLI install, no r…。

原文小纸条
  • **Hackable (git) install:** full source checkout, editable, best for contributors.
  • **npm install:** global CLI install, no repo, best for "just run it."
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Getting started, Updating. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Getting started, Updating. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can I switch between npm and git installs later?"> Yes. Install the other flavor, then run Doctor so…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I switch between npm and git installs later?"> Yes. Install the other flavor, then run Doctor so the gateway service points at the new entrypoint. This **does not delete your data** - it only changes the OpenClaw code install. Your state (~/.openclaw) and workspace (~/.openclaw/workspace) stay untouched.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“From npm to git:”。

原文小纸条

From npm to git:

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} git clone https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw.git cd openclaw pnpm install pnpm build openclaw doctor openclaw gateway restart ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“From git to npm:”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Doctor detects a 门口的小门卫 service entrypoint mismatch and offers to rewrite the service 设置说明书 to match the current instal…”。

原文小纸条

Doctor detects a gateway service entrypoint mismatch and offers to rewrite the service config to match the current install (use --repair in automation).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Backup tips: see Backup strategy. </Accordion>”。

Backup tips: see Backup strategy. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Should I run the 门口的小门卫 on my laptop or a VPS?"> Short answer: **if you want 24/7 reliability, use a…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Should I run the Gateway on my laptop or a VPS?"> Short answer: **if you want 24/7 reliability, use a VPS**. If you want the lowest friction and you're okay with sleep/restarts, run it locally.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“**Laptop (local 门口的小门卫)**”。

原文小纸条

**Laptop (local Gateway)**

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Pros:** no 大房子服务器 cost, direct access t…、**Cons:** sleep/network drops = disconnec…。

原文小纸条
  • **Pros:** no server cost, direct access to local files, live browser window.
  • **Cons:** sleep/network drops = disconnects, OS updates/reboots interrupt, must stay awake.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“**VPS / cloud**”。

原文小纸条

**VPS / cloud**

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Pros:** always-on, stable network, no l…、**Cons:** often run headless (use screens…。

原文小纸条
  • **Pros:** always-on, stable network, no laptop sleep issues, easier to keep running.
  • **Cons:** often run headless (use screenshots), remote file access only, you must SSH for updates.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“**OpenClaw-specific note:** WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack/Mattermost (新本领插件)/Discord all work fine from a VPS. The only real…”。

原文小纸条

**OpenClaw-specific note:** WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack/Mattermost (plugin)/Discord all work fine from a VPS. The only real trade-off is **headless browser** vs a visible window. See Browser.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“**Recommended default:** VPS if you had 门口的小门卫 disconnects before. Local is great when you're actively using the Mac an…”。

原文小纸条

**Recommended default:** VPS if you had gateway disconnects before. Local is great when you're actively using the Mac and want local file access or UI automation with a visible browser. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How important is it to run OpenClaw on a dedicated machine?"> Not required, but **recommended for rel…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How important is it to run OpenClaw on a dedicated machine?"> Not required, but **recommended for reliability and isolation**.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Dedicated host (VPS/Mac mini/Pi):** alw…、**Shared laptop/desktop:** totally fine f…。

原文小纸条
  • **Dedicated host (VPS/Mac mini/Pi):** always-on, fewer sleep/reboot interruptions, cleaner permissions, easier to keep running.
  • **Shared laptop/desktop:** totally fine for testing and active use, but expect pauses when the machine sleeps or updates.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If you want the best of both worlds, keep the 门口的小门卫 on a dedicated host and pair your laptop as a **node** for local s…”。

原文小纸条

If you want the best of both worlds, keep the Gateway on a dedicated host and pair your laptop as a **node** for local screen/camera/exec tools. See Nodes. For security guidance, read Security. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="What are the minimum VPS requirements and recommended OS?"> OpenClaw is lightweight. For a basic 门口的小…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="What are the minimum VPS requirements and recommended OS?"> OpenClaw is lightweight. For a basic Gateway + one chat channel:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Absolute minimum:** 1 vCPU, 1GB RAM, \~…、**Recommended:** 1-2 vCPU, 2GB RAM or mor…。

原文小纸条
  • **Absolute minimum:** 1 vCPU, 1GB RAM, \~500MB disk.
  • **Recommended:** 1-2 vCPU, 2GB RAM or more for headroom (logs, media, multiple channels). Node tools and browser automation can be resource hungry.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“OS: use **Ubuntu LTS** (or any modern Debian/Ubuntu). The Linux install path is best tested there.”。

原文小纸条

OS: use **Ubuntu LTS** (or any modern Debian/Ubuntu). The Linux install path is best tested there.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Linux, VPS hosting. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Linux, VPS hosting. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can I run OpenClaw in a VM and what are the requirements?"> Yes. Treat a VM the same as a VPS: it nee…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I run OpenClaw in a VM and what are the requirements?"> Yes. Treat a VM the same as a VPS: it needs to be always on, reachable, and have enough RAM for the Gateway and any channels you enable.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Baseline guidance:”。

原文小纸条

Baseline guidance:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Quick start and first-run setup”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Absolute minimum:** 1 vCPU, 1GB RAM.、**Recommended:** 2GB RAM or more if you r…、**OS:** Ubuntu LTS or another modern Debi…。

原文小纸条
  • **Absolute minimum:** 1 vCPU, 1GB RAM.
  • **Recommended:** 2GB RAM or more if you run multiple channels, browser automation, or media tools.
  • **OS:** Ubuntu LTS or another modern Debian/Ubuntu.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If you are on Windows, **WSL2 is the easiest VM style setup** and has the best 工具小帮手ing compatibility. See Windows, VPS…”。

原文小纸条

If you are on Windows, **WSL2 is the easiest VM style setup** and has the best tooling compatibility. See Windows, VPS hosting. If you are running macOS in a VM, see macOS VM. </Accordion> </AccordionGroup>

第 4 站

What is OpenClaw?

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“What is OpenClaw?”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="What is OpenClaw, in one paragraph?"> OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant you run…”。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="What is OpenClaw, in one paragraph?"> OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant you run on your own devices. It replies on the messaging surfaces you already use (WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Mattermost (plugin), Discord, Google Chat, Signal, iMessage, WebChat) and can also do voice + a live Canvas on supported platforms. The **Gateway** is the always-on control plane; the assistant is the product. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Value proposition"> OpenClaw is not "just a Claude wrapper." It's a **local-first control plane** tha…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Value proposition"> OpenClaw is not "just a Claude wrapper." It's a **local-first control plane** that lets you run a capable assistant on **your own hardware**, reachable from the chat apps you already use, with stateful sessions, memory, and tools - without handing control of your workflows to a hosted SaaS.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Highlights:”。

原文小纸条

Highlights:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“workspace + session history local.”。

原文小纸条

workspace + session history local.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“plus mobile voice and Canvas on supported platforms.”。

原文小纸条

plus mobile voice and Canvas on supported platforms.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“and failover.”。

原文小纸条

and failover.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“workspace and defaults.”。

原文小纸条

workspace and defaults.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“What is OpenClaw?”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Your devices, your data:** run the 门口的小…、**Real 消息通道s, not a web sandbox:** WhatsA…、**聪明脑袋模型-agnostic:** use Anthropic, OpenA…、**Local-only option:** run local 聪明脑袋模型s…。

原文小纸条
  • **Your devices, your data:** run the Gateway wherever you want (Mac, Linux, VPS) and keep the
  • **Real channels, not a web sandbox:** WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack/Discord/Signal/iMessage/etc,
  • **Model-agnostic:** use Anthropic, OpenAI, MiniMax, OpenRouter, etc., with per-agent routing
  • **Local-only option:** run local models so **all data can stay on your device** if you want.
  • **Multi-agent routing:** separate agents per channel, account, or task, each with its own
  • **Open source and hackable:** inspect, extend, and self-host without vendor lock-in.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: 门口的小门卫, 消息通道s, Multi-机器人朋友, Memory. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Gateway, Channels, Multi-agent, Memory. </Accordion>

像准备清单

这一组条目像“What is OpenClaw?”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Build a website (WordPress, Shopify, or a…、Prototype a mobile app (outline, screens,…、Organize files and folders (cleanup, nami…、Connect Gmail and automate summaries or f…。

原文小纸条
  • Build a website (WordPress, Shopify, or a simple static site).
  • Prototype a mobile app (outline, screens, API plan).
  • Organize files and folders (cleanup, naming, tagging).
  • Connect Gmail and automate summaries or follow ups.

It can handle large tasks, but it works best when you split them into phases and use sub agents for parallel work. </Accordion>

</Accordion>

像准备清单

这一组条目像“What is OpenClaw?”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Personal briefings:** summaries of inbo…、**Research and drafting:** quick research…、**Reminders and follow ups:** cron or hea…、**Browser 自动小闹钟:** filling forms, collect…。

原文小纸条
  • **Personal briefings:** summaries of inbox, calendar, and news you care about.
  • **Research and drafting:** quick research, summaries, and first drafts for emails or docs.
  • **Reminders and follow ups:** cron or heartbeat driven nudges and checklists.
  • **Browser automation:** filling forms, collecting data, and repeating web tasks.
  • **Cross device coordination:** send a task from your phone, let the Gateway run it on a server, and get the result back in chat.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can OpenClaw help with lead gen, outreach, ads, and blogs for a SaaS?"> Yes for **research, qualifica…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can OpenClaw help with lead gen, outreach, ads, and blogs for a SaaS?"> Yes for **research, qualification, and drafting**. It can scan sites, build shortlists, summarize prospects, and write outreach or ad copy drafts.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“For **outreach or ad runs**, keep a human in the loop. Avoid spam, follow local laws and platform policies, and review…”。

原文小纸条

For **outreach or ad runs**, keep a human in the loop. Avoid spam, follow local laws and platform policies, and review anything before it is sent. The safest pattern is to let OpenClaw draft and you approve.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: 安全守门员. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Security. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="What are the advantages vs Claude Code for web development?"> OpenClaw is a **personal assistant** an…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="What are the advantages vs Claude Code for web development?"> OpenClaw is a **personal assistant** and coordination layer, not an IDE replacement. Use Claude Code or Codex for the fastest direct coding loop inside a repo. Use OpenClaw when you want durable memory, cross-device access, and tool orchestration.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“What is OpenClaw?”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Persistent memory + workspace** across…、**Multi-platform access** (WhatsApp, Tele…、**工具小帮手 orchestration** (browser, files,…、**Always-on 门口的小门卫** (run on a VPS, inter…。

原文小纸条
  • **Persistent memory + workspace** across sessions
  • **Multi-platform access** (WhatsApp, Telegram, TUI, WebChat)
  • **Tool orchestration** (browser, files, scheduling, hooks)
  • **Always-on Gateway** (run on a VPS, interact from anywhere)
  • **Nodes** for local browser/screen/camera/exec

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Showcase: https://openclaw.ai/showcase </Accordion> </Accordion大家一起的房间>”。

第 5 站

Skills and 自动小闹钟

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Skills and 自动小闹钟”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="How do I customize skills without keeping the repo dirty?"> Use managed overrides…”。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="How do I customize skills without keeping the repo dirty?"> Use managed overrides instead of editing the repo copy. Put your changes in ~/.openclaw/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (or add a folder via skills.load.extraDirs in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json). Precedence is <workspace>/skills > ~/.openclaw/skills > bundled, so managed overrides win without touching git. Only upstream-worthy edits should live in the repo and go out as PRs. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can I load skills from a custom folder?"> Yes. Add extra directories via skills.load.extraDirs in ~/.…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I load skills from a custom folder?"> Yes. Add extra directories via skills.load.extraDirs in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json (lowest precedence). Default precedence remains: <workspace>/skills → ~/.openclaw/skills → bundled → skills.load.extraDirs. clawhub installs into ./skills by default, which OpenClaw treats as <workspace>/skills on the next session. </Accordion>

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Skills and automation”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Cron jobs**: isolated jobs can set a 聪明…、**Sub-机器人朋友s**: route tasks to separate 机…、**On-demand switch**: use /聪明脑袋模型 to swit…。

原文小纸条
  • **Cron jobs**: isolated jobs can set a model override per job.
  • **Sub-agents**: route tasks to separate agents with different default models.
  • **On-demand switch**: use /model to switch the current session model at any time.
像讲绘本

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="The bot freezes while doing heavy work. How do I offload that?"> Use **sub-机器人朋友s** for long or paral…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="The bot freezes while doing heavy work. How do I offload that?"> Use **sub-agents** for long or parallel tasks. Sub-agents run in their own session, return a summary, and keep your main chat responsive.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Ask your bot to "spawn a sub-机器人朋友 for this task" or use /sub机器人朋友s. Use /status in chat to see what the 门口的小门卫 is doin…”。

原文小纸条

Ask your bot to "spawn a sub-agent for this task" or use /subagents. Use /status in chat to see what the Gateway is doing right now (and whether it is busy).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Token tip: long tasks and sub-机器人朋友s both consume tokens. If cost is a concern, set a cheaper 聪明脑袋模型 for sub-机器人朋友s via…”。

原文小纸条

Token tip: long tasks and sub-agents both consume tokens. If cost is a concern, set a cheaper model for sub-agents via agents.defaults.subagents.model.

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do thread-bound sub机器人朋友 sessions work on Discord?"> Use thread bindings. You can bind a Discord…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do thread-bound subagent sessions work on Discord?"> Use thread bindings. You can bind a Discord thread to a subagent or session target so follow-up messages in that thread stay on that bound session.

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Basic flow:”。

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Skills and automation”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Spawn with sessions_spawn using thread: t…、Or manually bind with /focus <target>.、Use /机器人朋友s to inspect binding state.、Use /session idle <duration off> and /ses…。

原文小纸条
  • Spawn with sessions_spawn using thread: true (and optionally mode: "session" for persistent follow-up).
  • Or manually bind with /focus <target>.
  • Use /agents to inspect binding state.
  • Use /session idle <duration|off> and /session max-age <duration|off> to control auto-unfocus.
  • Use /unfocus to detach the thread.
像讲绘本

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Skills and automation”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Global defaults: session.threadBindings.e…、Discord overrides: 消息通道s.discord.threadBi…、Auto-bind on spawn: set 消息通道s.discord.thr…。

原文小纸条
  • Global defaults: session.threadBindings.enabled, session.threadBindings.idleHours, session.threadBindings.maxAgeHours.
  • Discord overrides: channels.discord.threadBindings.enabled, channels.discord.threadBindings.idleHours, channels.discord.threadBindings.maxAgeHours.
  • Auto-bind on spawn: set channels.discord.threadBindings.spawnSubagentSessions: true.

<Accordion title="Cron or reminders do not fire. What should I check?"> Cron runs inside the Gateway process. If the Gateway is not running continuously, scheduled jobs will not run.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Skills and automation”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Confirm cron is enabled (cron.enabled) an…、Check the 门口的小门卫 is running 24/7 (no slee…、Verify timezone settings for the job (--t…。

原文小纸条
  • Confirm cron is enabled (cron.enabled) and OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON is not set.
  • Check the Gateway is running 24/7 (no sleep/restarts).
  • Verify timezone settings for the job (--tz vs host timezone).

原文小纸条

原文小纸条

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Skills and automation”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Cron jobs** for scheduled or recurring…、**Heartbeat** for "main session" periodic…、**Isolated jobs** for autonomous 机器人朋友s t…。

原文小纸条
  • **Cron jobs** for scheduled or recurring tasks (persist across restarts).
  • **Heartbeat** for "main session" periodic checks.
  • **Isolated jobs** for autonomous agents that post summaries or deliver to chats.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Cron jobs, Cron vs Heartbeat, Heartbeat. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Cron jobs, Cron vs Heartbeat, Heartbeat. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can I run Apple macOS-only skills from Linux?"> Not directly. macOS skills are gated by metadata.open…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I run Apple macOS-only skills from Linux?"> Not directly. macOS skills are gated by metadata.openclaw.os plus required binaries, and skills only appear in the system prompt when they are eligible on the **Gateway host**. On Linux, darwin-only skills (like apple-notes, apple-reminders, things-mac) will not load unless you override the gating.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“You have three supported patterns:”。

原文小纸条

You have three supported patterns:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“**Option A - run the 门口的小门卫 on a Mac (simplest).** Run the 门口的小门卫 where the macOS binaries exist, then connect from Lin…”。

原文小纸条

**Option A - run the Gateway on a Mac (simplest).** Run the Gateway where the macOS binaries exist, then connect from Linux in remote mode or over Tailscale. The skills load normally because the Gateway host is macOS.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“**Option B - use a macOS node (no SSH).** Run the 门口的小门卫 on Linux, pair a macOS node (menubar app), and set **Node Run…”。

原文小纸条

**Option B - use a macOS node (no SSH).** Run the Gateway on Linux, pair a macOS node (menubar app), and set **Node Run Commands** to "Always Ask" or "Always Allow" on the Mac. OpenClaw can treat macOS-only skills as eligible when the required binaries exist on the node. The agent runs those skills via the nodes tool. If you choose "Always Ask", approving "Always Allow" in the prompt adds that command to the allowlist.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“**Option C - proxy macOS binaries over SSH (advanced).** Keep the 门口的小门卫 on Linux, but make the required CLI binaries r…”。

原文小纸条

**Option C - proxy macOS binaries over SSH (advanced).** Keep the Gateway on Linux, but make the required CLI binaries resolve to SSH wrappers that run on a Mac. Then override the skill to allow Linux so it stays eligible.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“1. Create an SSH wrapper for the binary (example: memo for Apple Notes):”。

原文小纸条

1. Create an SSH wrapper for the binary (example: memo for Apple Notes):

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} #!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail exec ssh -T user@mac-host /opt/homebrew/bin/memo "$@" ``

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Skills and automation”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Custom skill / 新本领插件:** best for reliab…、**Browser 自动小闹钟:** works without code but…。

原文小纸条
  • **Custom skill / plugin:** best for reliable API access (Notion/HeyGen both have APIs).
  • **Browser automation:** works without code but is slower and more fragile.

原文小纸条

If you want to keep context per client (agency workflows), a simple pattern is:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Skills and automation”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：One Notion page per 来帮忙的小伙伴 (context + pr…、Ask the 机器人朋友 to fetch that page at the s…。

原文小纸条
  • One Notion page per client (context + preferences + active work).
  • Ask the agent to fetch that page at the start of a session.

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Native installs land in the active workspace skills/ directory. For shared skills across 机器人朋友s, place them in ~/.openc…”。

原文小纸条

Native installs land in the active workspace skills/ directory. For shared skills across agents, place them in ~/.openclaw/skills/<name>/SKILL.md. Some skills expect binaries installed via Homebrew; on Linux that means Linuxbrew (see the Homebrew Linux FAQ entry above). See Skills and ClawHub. </Accordion>

第 6 站

Sandboxing and memory

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Sandboxing and memory”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="Is there a dedicated sandboxing doc?"> Yes. See Sandboxing. For Docker-specific se…”。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="Is there a dedicated sandboxing doc?"> Yes. See Sandboxing. For Docker-specific setup (full gateway in Docker or sandbox images), see Docker. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Docker feels limited - how do I enable full features?"> The default image is 安全守门员-first and runs as…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Docker feels limited - how do I enable full features?"> The default image is security-first and runs as the node user, so it does not include system packages, Homebrew, or bundled browsers. For a fuller setup:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“node /app/node_modules/playwright-core/cli.js install chromium”。

原文小纸条

node /app/node_modules/playwright-core/cli.js install chromium

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Sandboxing and memory”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Persist /home/node with OPENCLAW_HOME_VOL…、Bake system deps into the image with OPEN…、Install Playwright browsers via the bundl…、Set PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH and ensure t…。

原文小纸条
  • Persist /home/node with OPENCLAW_HOME_VOLUME so caches survive.
  • Bake system deps into the image with OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES.
  • Install Playwright browsers via the bundled CLI:
  • Set PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH and ensure the path is persisted.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Docker, Browser. </Accordion>”。

Docs: Docker, Browser. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can I keep DMs personal but make 大家一起的房间s public/sandboxed with one 机器人朋友?"> Yes - if your private tr…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I keep DMs personal but make groups public/sandboxed with one agent?"> Yes - if your private traffic is **DMs** and your public traffic is **groups**.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Use 机器人朋友s.defaults.sandbox.mode: "non-main" so 大家一起的房间/消息通道 sessions (non-main keys) run in Docker, while the main DM…”。

原文小纸条

Use agents.defaults.sandbox.mode: "non-main" so group/channel sessions (non-main keys) run in Docker, while the main DM session stays on-host. Then restrict what tools are available in sandboxed sessions via tools.sandbox.tools.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Setup walkthrough + example 设置说明书: 大家一起的房间s: personal DMs + public 大家一起的房间s”。

原文小纸条

Setup walkthrough + example config: Groups: personal DMs + public groups

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Key 设置说明书 reference: 门口的小门卫 设置说明书uration </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Key config reference: Gateway configuration </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do I bind a host folder into the sandbox?"> Set 机器人朋友s.defaults.sandbox.docker.binds to ["host:pa…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I bind a host folder into the sandbox?"> Set agents.defaults.sandbox.docker.binds to ["host:path:mode"] (e.g., "/home/user/src:/src:ro"). Global + per-agent binds merge; per-agent binds are ignored when scope: "shared". Use :ro for anything sensitive and remember binds bypass the sandbox filesystem walls. See Sandboxing and Sandbox vs Tool Policy vs Elevated for examples and safety notes. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How does memory work?"> OpenClaw memory is just Markdown files in the 机器人朋友 workspace:”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How does memory work?"> OpenClaw memory is just Markdown files in the agent workspace:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Sandboxing and memory”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Daily notes in memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md、Curated long-term notes in MEMORY.md (mai…。

原文小纸条
  • Daily notes in memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md
  • Curated long-term notes in MEMORY.md (main/private sessions only)
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“OpenClaw also runs a **silent pre-compaction memory flush** to remind the 聪明脑袋模型 to write durable notes before auto-com…”。

原文小纸条

OpenClaw also runs a **silent pre-compaction memory flush** to remind the model to write durable notes before auto-compaction. This only runs when the workspace is writable (read-only sandboxes skip it). See Memory. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Memory keeps forgetting things. How do I make it stick?"> Ask the bot to **write the fact to memory**…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Memory keeps forgetting things. How do I make it stick?"> Ask the bot to **write the fact to memory**. Long-term notes belong in MEMORY.md, short-term context goes into memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“This is still an area we are improving. It helps to remind the 聪明脑袋模型 to store memories; it will know what to do. If it…”。

原文小纸条

This is still an area we are improving. It helps to remind the model to store memories; it will know what to do. If it keeps forgetting, verify the Gateway is using the same workspace on every run.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Memory, 机器人朋友 workspace. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Memory, Agent workspace. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Does memory persist forever? What are the limits?"> Memory files live on disk and persist until you d…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Does memory persist forever? What are the limits?"> Memory files live on disk and persist until you delete them. The limit is your storage, not the model. The **session context** is still limited by the model context window, so long conversations can compact or truncate. That is why memory search exists - it pulls only the relevant parts back into context.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Memory, Context. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Memory, Context. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Does semantic memory search require an OpenAI 对话接口 key?"> Only if you use **OpenAI embeddings**. Code…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Does semantic memory search require an OpenAI API key?"> Only if you use **OpenAI embeddings**. Codex OAuth covers chat/completions and does **not** grant embeddings access, so **signing in with Codex (OAuth or the Codex CLI login)** does not help for semantic memory search. OpenAI embeddings still need a real API key (OPENAI_API_KEY or models.providers.openai.apiKey).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If you don't set a provider explicitly, OpenClaw auto-selects a provider when it can resolve an 对话接口 key (auth profiles…”。

原文小纸条

If you don't set a provider explicitly, OpenClaw auto-selects a provider when it can resolve an API key (auth profiles, models.providers.*.apiKey, or env vars). It prefers OpenAI if an OpenAI key resolves, otherwise Gemini if a Gemini key resolves, then Voyage, then Mistral. If no remote key is available, memory search stays disabled until you configure it. If you have a local model path configured and present, OpenClaw prefers local. Ollama is supported when you explicitly set memorySearch.provider = "ollama".

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If you'd rather stay local, set memorySearch.provider = "local" (and optionally memorySearch.fallback = "none"). If you…”。

原文小纸条

If you'd rather stay local, set memorySearch.provider = "local" (and optionally memorySearch.fallback = "none"). If you want Gemini embeddings, set memorySearch.provider = "gemini" and provide GEMINI_API_KEY (or memorySearch.remote.apiKey). We support **OpenAI, Gemini, Voyage, Mistral, Ollama, or local** embedding models - see Memory for the setup details. </Accordion> </AccordionGroup>

第 7 站

Where things live on disk

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Where things live on disk”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="Is all data used with OpenClaw saved locally?"> No - **OpenClaw's state is local**…”。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="Is all data used with OpenClaw saved locally?"> No - **OpenClaw's state is local**, but **external services still see what you send them**.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“(~/.openclaw + your workspace directory).”。

原文小纸条

(~/.openclaw + your workspace directory).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“their 对话接口s, and chat platforms (WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack/etc.) store 小纸条消息 data on their 大房子服务器s.”。

原文小纸条

their APIs, and chat platforms (WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack/etc.) store message data on their servers.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“traffic still goes through the 消息通道's 大房子服务器s.”。

原文小纸条

traffic still goes through the channel's servers.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Where things live on disk”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Local by default:** sessions, memory fi…、**Remote by necessity:** 小纸条消息s you send…、**You control the footprint:** using loca…。

原文小纸条
  • **Local by default:** sessions, memory files, config, and workspace live on the Gateway host
  • **Remote by necessity:** messages you send to model providers (Anthropic/OpenAI/etc.) go to
  • **You control the footprint:** using local models keeps prompts on your machine, but channel
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Related: 机器人朋友 workspace, Memory. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Related: Agent workspace, Memory. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Where does OpenClaw store its data?"> Everything lives under $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR (default: ~/.opencla…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Where does OpenClaw store its data?"> Everything lives under $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR (default: ~/.openclaw):

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Path Purpose --------------------------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------…”。

原文小纸条

| Path | Purpose | | --------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | | $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/openclaw.json | Main config (JSON5) | | $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/credentials/oauth.json | Legacy OAuth import (copied into auth profiles on first use) | | $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/agents/<agentId>/agent/auth-profiles.json | Auth profiles (OAuth, API keys, and optional keyRef/tokenRef) | | $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/secrets.json | Optional file-backed secret payload for file SecretRef providers | | $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/agents/<agentId>/agent/auth.json | Legacy compatibility file (static api_key entries scrubbed) | | $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/credentials/ | Provider state (e.g. whatsapp/<accountId>/creds.json) | | $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/agents/ | Per-agent state (agentDir + sessions) | | $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/agents/<agentId>/sessions/ | Conversation history & state (per agent) | | $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/agents/<agentId>/sessions/sessions.json | Session metadata (per agent) |

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Legacy single-机器人朋友 path: ~/.openclaw/机器人朋友/* (migrated by openclaw doctor).”。

原文小纸条

Legacy single-agent path: ~/.openclaw/agent/* (migrated by openclaw doctor).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Your **workspace** (机器人朋友S.md, memory files, skills, etc.) is separate and 设置说明书ured via 机器人朋友s.defaults.workspace (def…”。

原文小纸条

Your **workspace** (AGENTS.md, memory files, skills, etc.) is separate and configured via agents.defaults.workspace (default: ~/.openclaw/workspace). </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Where should 机器人朋友S.md / SOUL.md / USER.md / MEMORY.md live?"> These files live in the **机器人朋友 worksp…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Where should AGENTS.md / SOUL.md / USER.md / MEMORY.md live?"> These files live in the **agent workspace**, not ~/.openclaw.

MEMORY.md (or legacy fallback memory.md when MEMORY.md is absent), memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md, optional HEARTBEAT.md.

and shared skills (~/.openclaw/skills).

这一组条目像“Where things live on disk”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Workspace (per 机器人朋友)**: 机器人朋友S.md, SOU…、**State dir (~/.openclaw)**: 设置说明书, crede…。

原文小纸条
  • **Workspace (per agent)**: AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, IDENTITY.md, USER.md,
  • **State dir (~/.openclaw)**: config, credentials, auth profiles, sessions, logs,

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If the bot "forgets" after a restart, confirm the 门口的小门卫 is using the same workspace on every launch (and remember: rem…”。

原文小纸条

If the bot "forgets" after a restart, confirm the Gateway is using the same workspace on every launch (and remember: remote mode uses the **gateway host's** workspace, not your local laptop).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Tip: if you want a durable behavior or preference, ask the bot to **write it into 机器人朋友S.md or MEMORY.md** rather than…”。

原文小纸条

Tip: if you want a durable behavior or preference, ask the bot to **write it into AGENTS.md or MEMORY.md** rather than relying on chat history.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“See 机器人朋友 workspace and Memory. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

See Agent workspace and Memory. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Recommended backup strategy"> Put your **机器人朋友 workspace** in a **private** git repo and back it up s…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Recommended backup strategy"> Put your **agent workspace** in a **private** git repo and back it up somewhere private (for example GitHub private). This captures memory + AGENTS/SOUL/USER files, and lets you restore the assistant's "mind" later.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Do **not** commit anything under ~/.openclaw (credentials, sessions, tokens, or encrypted secrets payloads). If you nee…”。

原文小纸条

Do **not** commit anything under ~/.openclaw (credentials, sessions, tokens, or encrypted secrets payloads). If you need a full restore, back up both the workspace and the state directory separately (see the migration question above).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: 机器人朋友 workspace. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Agent workspace. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do I completely uninstall OpenClaw?"> See the dedicated guide: Uninstall. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I completely uninstall OpenClaw?"> See the dedicated guide: Uninstall. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can 机器人朋友s work outside the workspace?"> Yes. The workspace is the **default cwd** and memory anchor,…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can agents work outside the workspace?"> Yes. The workspace is the **default cwd** and memory anchor, not a hard sandbox. Relative paths resolve inside the workspace, but absolute paths can access other host locations unless sandboxing is enabled. If you need isolation, use agents.defaults.sandbox or per-agent sandbox settings. If you want a repo to be the default working directory, point that agent's workspace to the repo root. The OpenClaw repo is just source code; keep the workspace separate unless you intentionally want the agent to work inside it.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Example (repo as default cwd):”。

原文小纸条

Example (repo as default cwd):

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“</Accordion>”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="I'm in remote mode - where is the session store?"> Session state is owned by the **门口的小门卫 host**. If…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="I'm in remote mode - where is the session store?"> Session state is owned by the **gateway host**. If you're in remote mode, the session store you care about is on the remote machine, not your local laptop. See Session management. </Accordion> </AccordionGroup>

第 8 站

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="What format is the config? Where is it?"> OpenClaw reads an optional **JSON5** config from $OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH (default: ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json):
