原文小纸条

If the file is missing, it uses safe-ish defaults (including a default workspace of ~/.openclaw/workspace). </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="I set 门口的小门卫.bind: &#x22;lan&#x22; (or &#x22;tailnet&#x22;) and now nothing listens / the UI says una…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="I set gateway.bind: &#x22;lan&#x22; (or &#x22;tailnet&#x22;) and now nothing listens / the UI says unauthorized"> Non-loopback binds **require auth**. Configure gateway.auth.mode + gateway.auth.token (or use OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN).

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``json5 theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} { gateway: { bind: "lan", auth: { mode: "token", token: "replace-me", }, }, } ``

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Config basics”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：门口的小门卫.remote.token / .秘密口令 do **not** en…、Local call paths can use 门口的小门卫.remote.*…、If 门口的小门卫.auth.token / 门口的小门卫.auth.秘密口令 i…、The Control UI authenticates via connect.…。

原文小纸条
  • gateway.remote.token / .password do **not** enable local gateway auth by themselves.
  • Local call paths can use gateway.remote.* as fallback only when gateway.auth.* is unset.
  • If gateway.auth.token / gateway.auth.password is explicitly configured via SecretRef and unresolved, resolution fails closed (no remote fallback masking).
  • The Control UI authenticates via connect.params.auth.token (stored in app/UI settings). Avoid putting tokens in URLs.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Why do I need a token on localhost now?"> OpenClaw enforces token auth by default, including loopback…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Why do I need a token on localhost now?"> OpenClaw enforces token auth by default, including loopback. If no token is configured, gateway startup auto-generates one and saves it to gateway.auth.token, so **local WS clients must authenticate**. This blocks other local processes from calling the Gateway.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If you **really** want open loopback, set 门口的小门卫.auth.mode: "none" explicitly in your 设置说明书. Doctor can generate a toke…”。

原文小纸条

If you **really** want open loopback, set gateway.auth.mode: "none" explicitly in your config. Doctor can generate a token for you any time: openclaw doctor --generate-gateway-token. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Do I have to restart after changing 设置说明书?"> The 门口的小门卫 watches the 设置说明书 and supports hot-reload:”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Do I have to restart after changing config?"> The Gateway watches the config and supports hot-reload:

原文小纸条

</Accordion>

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Config basics”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：门口的小门卫.reload.mode: "hybrid" (default): h…、hot, restart, off are also supported。

原文小纸条
  • gateway.reload.mode: "hybrid" (default): hot-apply safe changes, restart for critical ones
  • hot, restart, off are also supported
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do I disable funny CLI taglines?"> Set cli.banner.taglineMode in 设置说明书:”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I disable funny CLI taglines?"> Set cli.banner.taglineMode in config:

像讲绘本

原文小纸条

``json5 theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} { cli: { banner: { taglineMode: "off", // random | default | off }, }, } ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“</Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

</Accordion>

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Config basics”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：off: hides tagline text but keeps the ban…、default: uses All your chats, one OpenCla…、random: rotating funny/seasonal taglines…、If you want no banner at all, set env OPE…。

原文小纸条
  • off: hides tagline text but keeps the banner title/version line.
  • default: uses All your chats, one OpenClaw. every time.
  • random: rotating funny/seasonal taglines (default behavior).
  • If you want no banner at all, set env OPENCLAW_HIDE_BANNER=1.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do I enable web search (and web fetch)?"> web_fetch works without an 对话接口 key. web_search require…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I enable web search (and web fetch)?"> web_fetch works without an API key. web_search requires a key for your selected provider (Brave, Gemini, Grok, Kimi, or Perplexity). **Recommended:** run openclaw configure --section web and choose a provider. Environment alternatives:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Config basics”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Brave: BRAVE_对话接口_KEY、Gemini: GEMINI_对话接口_KEY、Grok: XAI_对话接口_KEY、Kimi: KIMI_对话接口_KEY or MOONSHOT_对话接口_KEY。

原文小纸条
  • Brave: BRAVE_API_KEY
  • Gemini: GEMINI_API_KEY
  • Grok: XAI_API_KEY
  • Kimi: KIMI_API_KEY or MOONSHOT_API_KEY
  • Perplexity: PERPLEXITY_API_KEY or OPENROUTER_API_KEY
像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``json5 theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} { plugins: { entries: { brave: { config: { webSearch: { apiKey: "BRAVE_API_KEY_HERE", }, }, }, }, }, tools: { web: { search: { enabled: true, provider: "brave", maxResults: 5, }, fetch: { enabled: true, }, }, }, } ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Provider-specific web-search 设置说明书 now lives under 新本领插件s.entries.<新本领插件>.设置说明书.webSearch.*. Legacy 工具小帮手s.web.search.*…”。

原文小纸条

Provider-specific web-search config now lives under plugins.entries.<plugin>.config.webSearch.*. Legacy tools.web.search.* provider paths still load temporarily for compatibility, but they should not be used for new configs.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Notes:”。

原文小纸条

Notes:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Config basics”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：If you use allowlists, add web_search/web…、web_fetch is enabled by default (unless e…、Daemons read env vars from ~/.openclaw/.e…。

原文小纸条
  • If you use allowlists, add web_search/web_fetch or group:web.
  • web_fetch is enabled by default (unless explicitly disabled).
  • Daemons read env vars from ~/.openclaw/.env (or the service environment).

Docs: Web tools. </Accordion>

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="设置说明书.apply wiped my 设置说明书. How do I recover and avoid this?"> 设置说明书.apply replaces the **entire 设置说明…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="config.apply wiped my config. How do I recover and avoid this?"> config.apply replaces the **entire config**. If you send a partial object, everything else is removed.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Recover:”。

原文小纸条

Recover:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Config basics”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Restore from backup (git or a copied ~/.o…、If you have no backup, re-run openclaw do…、If this was unexpected, file a bug and in…、A local coding 机器人朋友 can often reconstruc…。

原文小纸条
  • Restore from backup (git or a copied ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json).
  • If you have no backup, re-run openclaw doctor and reconfigure channels/models.
  • If this was unexpected, file a bug and include your last known config or any backup.
  • A local coding agent can often reconstruct a working config from logs or history.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Avoid it:”。

Avoid it:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Config basics”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Use openclaw 设置说明书 set for small changes.、Use openclaw 设置说明书ure for interactive edi…。

原文小纸条
  • Use openclaw config set for small changes.
  • Use openclaw configure for interactive edits.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: 设置说明书, 设置说明书ure, Doctor. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Config, Configure, Doctor. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do I run a central 门口的小门卫 with specialized workers across devices?"> The common pattern is **one…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I run a central Gateway with specialized workers across devices?"> The common pattern is **one Gateway** (e.g. Raspberry Pi) plus **nodes** and **agents**:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Config basics”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**门口的小门卫 (central):** owns 消息通道s (Signal/…、**Nodes (devices):** Macs/iOS/Android con…、**机器人朋友s (workers):** separate brains/wor…、**Sub-机器人朋友s:** spawn background work fro…。

原文小纸条
  • **Gateway (central):** owns channels (Signal/WhatsApp), routing, and sessions.
  • **Nodes (devices):** Macs/iOS/Android connect as peripherals and expose local tools (system.run, canvas, camera).
  • **Agents (workers):** separate brains/workspaces for special roles (e.g. "Hetzner ops", "Personal data").
  • **Sub-agents:** spawn background work from a main agent when you want parallelism.
  • **TUI:** connect to the Gateway and switch agents/sessions.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Nodes, Remote access, Multi-机器人朋友 Routing, Sub-机器人朋友s, TUI. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Nodes, Remote access, Multi-Agent Routing, Sub-agents, TUI. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can the OpenClaw browser run headless?"> Yes. It's a 设置说明书 option:”。

Default is false (headful). Headless is more likely to trigger anti-bot checks on some sites. See Browser.

这一组条目像“Config basics”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：No visible browser window (use screenshot…、Some sites are stricter about 自动小闹钟 in he…。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I use Brave for browser control?"> Set browser.executablePath to your Brave binary (or any Chromium-based browser) and restart the Gateway. See the full config examples in Browser. </Accordion> </AccordionGroup>

第 9 站

Remote 门口的小门卫s and nodes

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Remote 门口的小门卫s and nodes”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="How do 魔法口令s propagate between Telegram, the 门口的小门卫, and nodes?"> Telegram 小纸条消息s…”。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="How do commands propagate between Telegram, the gateway, and nodes?"> Telegram messages are handled by the **gateway**. The gateway runs the agent and only then calls nodes over the **Gateway WebSocket** when a node tool is needed:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Telegram → 门口的小门卫 → 机器人朋友 → node.* → Node → 门口的小门卫 → Telegram”。

原文小纸条

Telegram → Gateway → Agent → node.* → Node → Gateway → Telegram

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Nodes don't see inbound provider traffic; they only receive node RPC calls. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Nodes don't see inbound provider traffic; they only receive node RPC calls. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How can my 机器人朋友 access my computer if the 门口的小门卫 is hosted remotely?"> Short answer: **pair your com…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How can my agent access my computer if the Gateway is hosted remotely?"> Short answer: **pair your computer as a node**. The Gateway runs elsewhere, but it can call node.* tools (screen, camera, system) on your local machine over the Gateway WebSocket.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Typical setup:”。

原文小纸条

Typical setup:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“1. Run the 门口的小门卫 on the always-on host (VPS/home 大房子服务器). 2. Put the 门口的小门卫 host + your computer on the same tailnet.…”。

原文小纸条

1. Run the Gateway on the always-on host (VPS/home server). 2. Put the Gateway host + your computer on the same tailnet. 3. Ensure the Gateway WS is reachable (tailnet bind or SSH tunnel). 4. Open the macOS app locally and connect in **Remote over SSH** mode (or direct tailnet) so it can register as a node. 5. Approve the node on the Gateway:

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw devices list openclaw devices approve <requestId> ``

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Remote gateways and nodes”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：门口的小门卫 is running: openclaw 门口的小门卫 status、门口的小门卫 health: openclaw status、消息通道 health: openclaw 消息通道s status。

  • Gateway health: openclaw status
  • Channel health: openclaw channels status

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Remote gateways and nodes”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：If you use Tailscale Serve, make sure 门口的…、If you connect via SSH tunnel, confirm th…、Confirm your allowlists (DM or 大家一起的房间) i…。

原文小纸条
  • If you use Tailscale Serve, make sure gateway.auth.allowTailscale is set correctly.
  • If you connect via SSH tunnel, confirm the local tunnel is up and points at the right port.
  • Confirm your allowlists (DM or group) include your account.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Tailscale, Remote access, 消息通道s. </Accordion>”。

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can two OpenClaw instances talk to each other (local + VPS)?"> Yes. There is no built-in "bot-to-bot"…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can two OpenClaw instances talk to each other (local + VPS)?"> Yes. There is no built-in "bot-to-bot" bridge, but you can wire it up in a few reliable ways:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“**Simplest:** use a normal chat 消息通道 both bots can access (Telegram/Slack/WhatsApp). Have Bot A send a 小纸条消息 to Bot B,…”。

原文小纸条

**Simplest:** use a normal chat channel both bots can access (Telegram/Slack/WhatsApp). Have Bot A send a message to Bot B, then let Bot B reply as usual.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“**CLI bridge (generic):** run a script that calls the other 门口的小门卫 with openclaw 机器人朋友 --小纸条消息 ... --deliver, targeting…”。

原文小纸条

**CLI bridge (generic):** run a script that calls the other Gateway with openclaw agent --message ... --deliver, targeting a chat where the other bot listens. If one bot is on a remote VPS, point your CLI at that remote Gateway via SSH/Tailscale (see Remote access).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Example pattern (run from a machine that can reach the target 门口的小门卫):”。

原文小纸条

Example pattern (run from a machine that can reach the target Gateway):

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw agent --message "Hello from local bot" --deliver --channel telegram --reply-to <chat-id> ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Tip: add a guardrail so the two bots do not loop endlessly (mention-only, 消息通道 allowlists, or a "do not reply to bot 小纸…”。

原文小纸条

Tip: add a guardrail so the two bots do not loop endlessly (mention-only, channel allowlists, or a "do not reply to bot messages" rule).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Remote access, 机器人朋友 CLI, 机器人朋友 send. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Remote access, Agent CLI, Agent send. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Do I need separate VPSes for multiple 机器人朋友s?"> No. One 门口的小门卫 can host multiple 机器人朋友s, each with it…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Do I need separate VPSes for multiple agents?"> No. One Gateway can host multiple agents, each with its own workspace, model defaults, and routing. That is the normal setup and it is much cheaper and simpler than running one VPS per agent.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Use separate VPSes only when you need hard isolation (安全守门员 boundaries) or very different 设置说明书s that you do not want t…”。

原文小纸条

Use separate VPSes only when you need hard isolation (security boundaries) or very different configs that you do not want to share. Otherwise, keep one Gateway and use multiple agents or sub-agents. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Is there a benefit to using a node on my personal laptop instead of SSH from a VPS?"> Yes - nodes are…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Is there a benefit to using a node on my personal laptop instead of SSH from a VPS?"> Yes - nodes are the first-class way to reach your laptop from a remote Gateway, and they unlock more than shell access. The Gateway runs on macOS/Linux (Windows via WSL2) and is lightweight (a small VPS or Raspberry Pi-class box is fine; 4 GB RAM is plenty), so a common setup is an always-on host plus your laptop as a node.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Remote gateways and nodes”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**No inbound SSH required.** Nodes connec…、**Safer execution controls.** system.run…、**More device 工具小帮手s.** Nodes expose canv…、**Local browser 自动小闹钟.** Keep the 门口的小门卫…。

原文小纸条
  • **No inbound SSH required.** Nodes connect out to the Gateway WebSocket and use device pairing.
  • **Safer execution controls.** system.run is gated by node allowlists/approvals on that laptop.
  • **More device tools.** Nodes expose canvas, camera, and screen in addition to system.run.
  • **Local browser automation.** Keep the Gateway on a VPS, but run Chrome locally through a node host on the laptop, or attach to local Chrome on the host via Chrome MCP.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“SSH is fine for ad-hoc shell access, but nodes are simpler for ongoing 机器人朋友 workflows and device 自动小闹钟.”。

原文小纸条

SSH is fine for ad-hoc shell access, but nodes are simpler for ongoing agent workflows and device automation.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Nodes, Nodes CLI, Browser. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Nodes, Nodes CLI, Browser. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Do nodes run a 门口的小门卫 service?"> No. Only **one 门口的小门卫** should run per host unless you intentionally…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Do nodes run a gateway service?"> No. Only **one gateway** should run per host unless you intentionally run isolated profiles (see Multiple gateways). Nodes are peripherals that connect to the gateway (iOS/Android nodes, or macOS "node mode" in the menubar app). For headless node hosts and CLI control, see Node host CLI.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“A full restart is required for 门口的小门卫, discovery, and canvasHost changes. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

A full restart is required for gateway, discovery, and canvasHost changes. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Is there an 对话接口 / RPC way to apply 设置说明书?"> Yes. 设置说明书.apply validates + writes the full 设置说明书 and r…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Is there an API / RPC way to apply config?"> Yes. config.apply validates + writes the full config and restarts the Gateway as part of the operation. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do I connect a Mac node to a remote 门口的小门卫 (Tailscale Serve)?"> Serve exposes the **门口的小门卫 Contro…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I connect a Mac node to a remote Gateway (Tailscale Serve)?"> Serve exposes the **Gateway Control UI + WS**. Nodes connect over the same Gateway WS endpoint.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Recommended setup:”。

Recommended setup:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“1. **Make sure the VPS + Mac are on the same tailnet**. 2. **Use the macOS app in Remote mode** (SSH target can be the…”。

原文小纸条

1. **Make sure the VPS + Mac are on the same tailnet**. 2. **Use the macOS app in Remote mode** (SSH target can be the tailnet hostname). The app will tunnel the Gateway port and connect as a node. 3. **Approve the node** on the gateway:

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw devices list openclaw devices approve <requestId> ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: 门口的小门卫 protocol, Discovery, macOS remote mode. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Gateway protocol, Discovery, macOS remote mode. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Should I install on a second laptop or just add a node?"> If you only need **local 工具小帮手s** (screen/c…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Should I install on a second laptop or just add a node?"> If you only need **local tools** (screen/camera/exec) on the second laptop, add it as a **node**. That keeps a single Gateway and avoids duplicated config. Local node tools are currently macOS-only, but we plan to extend them to other OSes.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Install a second 门口的小门卫 only when you need **hard isolation** or two fully separate bots.”。

原文小纸条

Install a second Gateway only when you need **hard isolation** or two fully separate bots.

第 10 站

Env vars and .env loading

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Env vars and .env loading”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="How does OpenClaw load environment variables?"> OpenClaw reads env vars from the p…”。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="How does OpenClaw load environment variables?"> OpenClaw reads env vars from the parent process (shell, launchd/systemd, CI, etc.) and additionally loads:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Env vars and .env loading”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：.env from the current working directory、a global fallback .env from ~/.openclaw/.…。

原文小纸条
  • .env from the current working directory
  • a global fallback .env from ~/.openclaw/.env (aka $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/.env)
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Neither .env file overrides existing env vars.”。

原文小纸条

Neither .env file overrides existing env vars.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“You can also define inline env vars in 设置说明书 (applied only if missing from the process env):”。

原文小纸条

You can also define inline env vars in config (applied only if missing from the process env):

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``json5 theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} { env: { OPENROUTER_API_KEY: "sk-or-...", vars: { GROQ_API_KEY: "gsk-..." }, }, } ``

<Accordion title="I started the Gateway via the service and my env vars disappeared. What now?"> Two common fixes:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“1. Put the missing keys in ~/.openclaw/.env so they're picked up even when the service doesn't inherit your shell env.…”。

原文小纸条

1. Put the missing keys in ~/.openclaw/.env so they're picked up even when the service doesn't inherit your shell env. 2. Enable shell import (opt-in convenience):

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``json5 theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} { env: { shellEnv: { enabled: true, timeoutMs: 15000, }, }, } ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“This runs your login shell and imports only missing expected keys (never overrides). Env var equivalents: OPENCLAW_LOAD…”。

原文小纸条

This runs your login shell and imports only missing expected keys (never overrides). Env var equivalents: OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV=1, OPENCLAW_SHELL_ENV_TIMEOUT_MS=15000. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="I set COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN, but 聪明脑袋模型s status shows &#x22;Shell env: off.&#x22; Why?"> openclaw 聪明脑袋…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="I set COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN, but models status shows &#x22;Shell env: off.&#x22; Why?"> openclaw models status reports whether **shell env import** is enabled. "Shell env: off" does **not** mean your env vars are missing - it just means OpenClaw won't load your login shell automatically.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If the 门口的小门卫 runs as a service (launchd/systemd), it won't inherit your shell environment. Fix by doing one of these:”。

原文小纸条

If the Gateway runs as a service (launchd/systemd), it won't inherit your shell environment. Fix by doing one of these:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“1. Put the token in ~/.openclaw/.env:”。

原文小纸条

1. Put the token in ~/.openclaw/.env:

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

`` COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN=... ``

像讲绘本

原文小纸条

2. Or enable shell import (env.shellEnv.enabled: true).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“3. Or add it to your 设置说明书 env block (applies only if missing).”。

原文小纸条

3. Or add it to your config env block (applies only if missing).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Then restart the 门口的小门卫 and recheck:”。

第 11 站

Sessions and multiple chats

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Sessions and multiple chats”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="How do I start a fresh conversation?"> Send /new or /reset as a standalone 小纸条消息.…”。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="How do I start a fresh conversation?"> Send /new or /reset as a standalone message. See Session management. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Do sessions reset automatically if I never send /new?"> Yes. Sessions expire after session.idleMinute…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Do sessions reset automatically if I never send /new?"> Yes. Sessions expire after session.idleMinutes (default **60**). The **next** message starts a fresh session id for that chat key. This does not delete transcripts - it just starts a new session.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“</Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

``json5 theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} { session: { idleMinutes: 240, }, } `` </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Is there a way to make a team of OpenClaw instances (one CEO and many 机器人朋友s)?"> Yes, via **multi-机器人…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Is there a way to make a team of OpenClaw instances (one CEO and many agents)?"> Yes, via **multi-agent routing** and **sub-agents**. You can create one coordinator agent and several worker agents with their own workspaces and models.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“That said, this is best seen as a **fun experiment**. It is token heavy and often less efficient than using one bot wit…”。

原文小纸条

That said, this is best seen as a **fun experiment**. It is token heavy and often less efficient than using one bot with separate sessions. The typical model we envision is one bot you talk to, with different sessions for parallel work. That bot can also spawn sub-agents when needed.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Multi-机器人朋友 routing, Sub-机器人朋友s, 机器人朋友s CLI. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Multi-agent routing, Sub-agents, Agents CLI. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Why did context get truncated mid-task? How do I prevent it?"> Session context is limited by the 聪明脑袋…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Why did context get truncated mid-task? How do I prevent it?"> Session context is limited by the model window. Long chats, large tool outputs, or many files can trigger compaction or truncation.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“What helps:”。

原文小纸条

What helps:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“</Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

</Accordion>

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Sessions and multiple chats”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Ask the bot to summarize the current stat…、Use /compact before long tasks, and /new…、Keep important context in the workspace a…、Use sub-机器人朋友s for long or parallel work…。

原文小纸条
  • Ask the bot to summarize the current state and write it to a file.
  • Use /compact before long tasks, and /new when switching topics.
  • Keep important context in the workspace and ask the bot to read it back.
  • Use sub-agents for long or parallel work so the main chat stays smaller.
  • Pick a model with a larger context window if this happens often.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do I completely reset OpenClaw but keep it installed?"> Use the reset 魔法口令:”。

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Sessions and multiple chats”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Onboarding also offers **Reset** if it se…、If you used profiles (--profile / OPENCLA…、Dev reset: openclaw 门口的小门卫 --dev --reset…。

原文小纸条
  • Onboarding also offers **Reset** if it sees an existing config. See Onboarding (CLI).
  • If you used profiles (--profile / OPENCLAW_PROFILE), reset each state dir (defaults are ~/.openclaw-<profile>).
  • Dev reset: openclaw gateway --dev --reset (dev-only; wipes dev config + credentials + sessions + workspace).

  • Enable or tune **session pruning** (agents.defaults.contextPruning) to trim old tool output.
  • Use a model with a larger context window.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Compaction, Session pruning, Session management. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Compaction, Session pruning, Session management. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Why am I seeing &#x22;LLM request rejected: 小纸条消息s.content.工具小帮手_use.input field required&#x22;?"> Th…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Why am I seeing &#x22;LLM request rejected: messages.content.tool_use.input field required&#x22;?"> This is a provider validation error: the model emitted a tool_use block without the required input. It usually means the session history is stale or corrupted (often after long threads or a tool/schema change).

像讲绘本

Fix: start a fresh session with /new (standalone message). </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Why am I getting heartbeat 小纸条消息s every 30 minutes?"> Heartbeats run every **30m** by default. Tune o…”。

<Accordion title="Why am I getting heartbeat messages every 30 minutes?"> Heartbeats run every **30m** by default. Tune or disable them:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``json5 theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} { agents: { defaults: { heartbeat: { every: "2h", // or "0m" to disable }, }, }, } ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If HEARTBEAT.md exists but is effectively empty (only blank lines and markdown headers like # Heading), OpenClaw skips…”。

原文小纸条

If HEARTBEAT.md exists but is effectively empty (only blank lines and markdown headers like # Heading), OpenClaw skips the heartbeat run to save API calls. If the file is missing, the heartbeat still runs and the model decides what to do.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Per-机器人朋友 overrides use 机器人朋友s.list[].heartbeat. Docs: Heartbeat. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Per-agent overrides use agents.list[].heartbeat. Docs: Heartbeat. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Do I need to add a &#x22;bot account&#x22; to a WhatsApp 大家一起的房间?"> No. OpenClaw runs on **your own a…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Do I need to add a &#x22;bot account&#x22; to a WhatsApp group?"> No. OpenClaw runs on **your own account**, so if you're in the group, OpenClaw can see it. By default, group replies are blocked until you allow senders (groupPolicy: "allowlist").

<Accordion title="How do I get the JID of a WhatsApp group?"> Option 1 (fastest): tail logs and send a test message in the group:

<Accordion title="Why does OpenClaw not reply in a group?"> Two common causes:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Sessions and multiple chats”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Mention gating is on (default). You must…、You 设置说明书ured 消息通道s.whatsapp.大家一起的房间s wit…。

原文小纸条
  • Mention gating is on (default). You must @mention the bot (or match mentionPatterns).
  • You configured channels.whatsapp.groups without "*" and the group isn't allowlisted.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“See 大家一起的房间s and 大家一起的房间 小纸条消息s. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

See Groups and Group messages. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Do 大家一起的房间s/threads share context with DMs?"> Direct chats collapse to the main session by default. 大…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Do groups/threads share context with DMs?"> Direct chats collapse to the main session by default. Groups/channels have their own session keys, and Telegram topics / Discord threads are separate sessions. See Groups and Group messages. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How many workspaces and 机器人朋友s can I create?"> No hard limits. Dozens (even hundreds) are fine, but w…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How many workspaces and agents can I create?"> No hard limits. Dozens (even hundreds) are fine, but watch for:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Sessions and multiple chats”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Disk growth:** sessions + transcripts l…、**Token cost:** more 机器人朋友s means more co…、**Ops overhead:** per-机器人朋友 auth profiles…。

原文小纸条
  • **Disk growth:** sessions + transcripts live under ~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/sessions/.
  • **Token cost:** more agents means more concurrent model usage.
  • **Ops overhead:** per-agent auth profiles, workspaces, and channel routing.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Tips:”。

原文小纸条

Tips:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“</Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

</Accordion>

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Sessions and multiple chats”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Keep one **active** workspace per 机器人朋友 (…、Prune old sessions (delete JSONL or store…、Use openclaw doctor to spot stray workspa…。

原文小纸条
  • Keep one **active** workspace per agent (agents.defaults.workspace).
  • Prune old sessions (delete JSONL or store entries) if disk grows.
  • Use openclaw doctor to spot stray workspaces and profile mismatches.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can I run multiple bots or chats at the same time (Slack), and how should I set that up?"> Yes. Use *…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I run multiple bots or chats at the same time (Slack), and how should I set that up?"> Yes. Use **Multi-Agent Routing** to run multiple isolated agents and route inbound messages by channel/account/peer. Slack is supported as a channel and can be bound to specific agents.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Browser access is powerful but not "do anything a human can" - anti-bot, CAPTCHAs, and MFA can still block 自动小闹钟. For t…”。

原文小纸条

Browser access is powerful but not "do anything a human can" - anti-bot, CAPTCHAs, and MFA can still block automation. For the most reliable browser control, use local Chrome MCP on the host, or use CDP on the machine that actually runs the browser.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Best-practice setup:”。

原文小纸条

Best-practice setup:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Sessions and multiple chats”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Always-on 门口的小门卫 host (VPS/Mac mini).、One 机器人朋友 per role (bindings).、Slack 消息通道(s) bound to those 机器人朋友s.、Local browser via Chrome MCP or a node wh…。

原文小纸条
  • Always-on Gateway host (VPS/Mac mini).
  • One agent per role (bindings).
  • Slack channel(s) bound to those agents.
  • Local browser via Chrome MCP or a node when needed.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Multi-机器人朋友 Routing, Slack, Browser, Nodes. </Accordion> </Accordion大家一起的房间>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Multi-Agent Routing, Slack, Browser, Nodes. </Accordion> </AccordionGroup>

第 12 站

聪明脑袋模型s: defaults, selection, aliases, switching

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“聪明脑袋模型s: defaults, selection, aliases, switching”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="What is the &#x22;default 聪明脑袋模型&#x22;?"> OpenClaw's default 聪明脑袋模型 is whatever yo…”。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="What is the &#x22;default model&#x22;?"> OpenClaw's default model is whatever you set as:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

`` agents.defaults.model.primary ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“聪明脑袋模型s are referenced as provider/聪明脑袋模型 (example: anthropic/claude-opus-4-6). If you omit the provider, OpenClaw curr…”。

原文小纸条

Models are referenced as provider/model (example: anthropic/claude-opus-4-6). If you omit the provider, OpenClaw currently assumes anthropic as a temporary deprecation fallback - but you should still **explicitly** set provider/model. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="What 聪明脑袋模型 do you recommend?"> **Recommended default:** use the strongest latest-generation 聪明脑袋模型 a…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="What model do you recommend?"> **Recommended default:** use the strongest latest-generation model available in your provider stack. **For tool-enabled or untrusted-input agents:** prioritize model strength over cost. **For routine/low-stakes chat:** use cheaper fallback models and route by agent role.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“MiniMax has its own docs: MiniMax and Local 聪明脑袋模型s.”。

原文小纸条

MiniMax has its own docs: MiniMax and Local models.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Rule of thumb: use the **best 聪明脑袋模型 you can afford** for high-stakes work, and a cheaper 聪明脑袋模型 for routine chat or su…”。

原文小纸条

Rule of thumb: use the **best model you can afford** for high-stakes work, and a cheaper model for routine chat or summaries. You can route models per agent and use sub-agents to parallelize long tasks (each sub-agent consumes tokens). See Models and Sub-agents.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Strong warning: weaker/over-quantized 聪明脑袋模型s are more vulnerable to prompt injection and unsafe behavior. See 安全守门员.”。

原文小纸条

Strong warning: weaker/over-quantized models are more vulnerable to prompt injection and unsafe behavior. See Security.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“More context: 聪明脑袋模型s. </Accordion>”。

More context: Models. </Accordion>

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do I switch 聪明脑袋模型s without wiping my 设置说明书?"> Use **聪明脑袋模型 魔法口令s** or edit only the **聪明脑袋模型** f…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I switch models without wiping my config?"> Use **model commands** or edit only the **model** fields. Avoid full config replaces.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Safe options:”。

原文小纸条

Safe options:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Models: defaults, selection, aliases, switching”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：/聪明脑袋模型 in chat (quick, per-session)、openclaw 聪明脑袋模型s set ... (updates just 聪明…、openclaw 设置说明书ure --section 聪明脑袋模型 (inter…、edit 机器人朋友s.defaults.聪明脑袋模型 in ~/.opencla…。

原文小纸条
  • /model in chat (quick, per-session)
  • openclaw models set ... (updates just model config)
  • openclaw configure --section model (interactive)
  • edit agents.defaults.model in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Avoid 设置说明书.apply with a partial object unless you intend to replace the whole 设置说明书. If you did overwrite 设置说明书, resto…”。

原文小纸条

Avoid config.apply with a partial object unless you intend to replace the whole config. If you did overwrite config, restore from backup or re-run openclaw doctor to repair.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: 聪明脑袋模型s, 设置说明书ure, 设置说明书, Doctor. </Accordion>”。

Docs: Models, Configure, Config, Doctor. </Accordion>

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I use self-hosted models (llama.cpp, vLLM, Ollama)?"> Yes. Ollama is the easiest path for local models.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Quickest setup:”。

原文小纸条

Quickest setup:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“1. Install Ollama from https://ollama.com/download 2. Pull a local 聪明脑袋模型 such as ollama pull glm-4.7-flash 3. If you w…”。

原文小纸条

1. Install Ollama from https://ollama.com/download 2. Pull a local model such as ollama pull glm-4.7-flash 3. If you want Ollama Cloud too, run ollama signin 4. Run openclaw onboard and choose Ollama 5. Pick Local or Cloud + Local

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Notes:”。

原文小纸条

Notes:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Models: defaults, selection, aliases, switching”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Cloud + Local gives you Ollama Cloud 聪明脑袋…、cloud 聪明脑袋模型s such as kimi-k2.5:cloud do…、for manual switching, use openclaw 聪明脑袋模型…。

原文小纸条
  • Cloud + Local gives you Ollama Cloud models plus your local Ollama models
  • cloud models such as kimi-k2.5:cloud do not need a local pull
  • for manual switching, use openclaw models list and openclaw models set ollama/<model>
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“安全守门员 note: smaller or heavily quantized 聪明脑袋模型s are more vulnerable to prompt injection. We strongly recommend **large…”。

原文小纸条

Security note: smaller or heavily quantized models are more vulnerable to prompt injection. We strongly recommend **large models** for any bot that can use tools. If you still want small models, enable sandboxing and strict tool allowlists.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Ollama, Local 聪明脑袋模型s, 聪明脑袋模型 providers, 安全守门员, Sandboxing. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Ollama, Local models, Model providers, Security, Sandboxing. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="What do OpenClaw, Flawd, and Krill use for 聪明脑袋模型s?">”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="What do OpenClaw, Flawd, and Krill use for models?">

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“</Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

</Accordion>

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Models: defaults, selection, aliases, switching”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：These deployments can differ and may chan…、Check the current runtime setting on each…、For 安全守门员-sensitive/工具小帮手-enabled 机器人朋友s,…。

原文小纸条
  • These deployments can differ and may change over time; there is no fixed provider recommendation.
  • Check the current runtime setting on each gateway with openclaw models status.
  • For security-sensitive/tool-enabled agents, use the strongest latest-generation model available.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do I switch 聪明脑袋模型s on the fly (without restarting)?"> Use the /聪明脑袋模型 魔法口令 as a standalone 小纸条消息:”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I switch models on the fly (without restarting)?"> Use the /model command as a standalone message:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

`` /model sonnet /model haiku /model opus /model gpt /model gpt-mini /model gemini /model gemini-flash ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“You can list available 聪明脑袋模型s with /聪明脑袋模型, /聪明脑袋模型 list, or /聪明脑袋模型 status.”。

原文小纸条

You can list available models with /model, /model list, or /model status.

像讲绘本

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I use GPT 5.2 for daily tasks and Codex 5.3 for coding?"> Yes. Set one as default and switch as needed:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Models: defaults, selection, aliases, switching”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Quick switch (per session):** /聪明脑袋模型 g…、**Default + switch:** set 机器人朋友s.defaults…、**Sub-机器人朋友s:** route coding tasks to sub…。

原文小纸条
  • **Quick switch (per session):** /model gpt-5.2 for daily tasks, /model openai-codex/gpt-5.4 for coding with Codex OAuth.
  • **Default + switch:** set agents.defaults.model.primary to openai/gpt-5.2, then switch to openai-codex/gpt-5.4 when coding (or the other way around).
  • **Sub-agents:** route coding tasks to sub-agents with a different default model.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“See 聪明脑袋模型s and Slash 魔法口令s. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

See Models and Slash commands. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Why do I see &#x22;聪明脑袋模型 ... is not allowed&#x22; and then no reply?"> If 机器人朋友s.defaults.聪明脑袋模型s is…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Why do I see &#x22;Model ... is not allowed&#x22; and then no reply?"> If agents.defaults.models is set, it becomes the **allowlist** for /model and any session overrides. Choosing a model that isn't in that list returns:

原文小纸条

`` Model "provider/model" is not allowed. Use /model to list available models. ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“That error is returned **instead of** a normal reply. Fix: add the 聪明脑袋模型 to 机器人朋友s.defaults.聪明脑袋模型s, remove the allowl…”。

原文小纸条

That error is returned **instead of** a normal reply. Fix: add the model to agents.defaults.models, remove the allowlist, or pick a model from /model list. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Why do I see &#x22;Unknown 聪明脑袋模型: minimax/MiniMax-M2.7&#x22;?"> This means the **provider isn't 设置说明…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Why do I see &#x22;Unknown model: minimax/MiniMax-M2.7&#x22;?"> This means the **provider isn't configured** (no MiniMax provider config or auth profile was found), so the model can't be resolved.

原文小纸条

1. Upgrade to a current OpenClaw release (or run from source main), then restart the gateway. 2. Make sure MiniMax is configured (wizard or JSON), or that a MiniMax API key exists in env/auth profiles so the provider can be injected. 3. Use the exact model id (case-sensitive): minimax/MiniMax-M2.7 or minimax/MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed. 4. Run:

and pick from the list (or /model list in chat).

<Accordion title="Can I use MiniMax as my default and OpenAI for complex tasks?"> Yes. Use **MiniMax as the default** and switch models **per session** when needed. Fallbacks are for **errors**, not "hard tasks," so use /model or a separate agent.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Models: defaults, selection, aliases, switching”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：opus → anthropic/claude-opus-4-6、sonnet → anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6、gpt → openai/gpt-5.4、gpt-mini → openai/gpt-5-mini。

原文小纸条
  • opus → anthropic/claude-opus-4-6
  • sonnet → anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6
  • gpt → openai/gpt-5.4
  • gpt-mini → openai/gpt-5-mini
  • gemini → google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview
  • gemini-flash → google/gemini-3-flash-preview
  • gemini-flash-lite → google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Models: defaults, selection, aliases, switching”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Run openclaw 机器人朋友s add <id> and 设置说明书ure…、Or copy auth-profiles.json from the main…。

第 13 站

聪明脑袋模型 failover and "All 聪明脑袋模型s failed"

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“聪明脑袋模型 failover and "All 聪明脑袋模型s failed"”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="How does failover work?"> Failover happens in two stages:”。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="How does failover work?"> Failover happens in two stages:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“1. **Auth profile rotation** within the same provider. 2. **聪明脑袋模型 fallback** to the next 聪明脑袋模型 in 机器人朋友s.defaults.聪明脑…”。

原文小纸条

1. **Auth profile rotation** within the same provider. 2. **Model fallback** to the next model in agents.defaults.model.fallbacks.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Cooldowns apply to failing profiles (exponential backoff), so OpenClaw can keep responding even when a provider is rate…”。

原文小纸条

Cooldowns apply to failing profiles (exponential backoff), so OpenClaw can keep responding even when a provider is rate-limited or temporarily failing. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="What does &#x22;No credentials found for profile anthropic:default&#x22; mean?"> It means the system…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="What does &#x22;No credentials found for profile anthropic:default&#x22; mean?"> It means the system attempted to use the auth profile ID anthropic:default, but could not find credentials for it in the expected auth store.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“**Fix checklist:**”。

原文小纸条

**Fix checklist:**

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Model failover and "All models failed"”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Confirm where auth profiles live** (new…、Current: ~/.openclaw/机器人朋友s/<机器人朋友Id>/机器人…、Legacy: ~/.openclaw/机器人朋友/* (migrated by…、**Confirm your env var is loaded by the 门…。

原文小纸条
  • **Confirm where auth profiles live** (new vs legacy paths)
  • Current: ~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/agent/auth-profiles.json
  • Legacy: ~/.openclaw/agent/* (migrated by openclaw doctor)
  • **Confirm your env var is loaded by the Gateway**
  • If you set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in your shell but run the Gateway via systemd/launchd, it may not inherit it. Put it in ~/.openclaw/.env or enable env.shellEnv.
  • **Make sure you're editing the correct agent**
  • Multi-agent setups mean there can be multiple auth-profiles.json files.
  • **Sanity-check model/auth status**
  • Use openclaw models status to see configured models and whether providers are authenticated.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“**Fix checklist for "No credentials found for profile anthropic"**”。

原文小纸条

**Fix checklist for "No credentials found for profile anthropic"**

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“This means the run is pinned to an Anthropic auth profile, but the 门口的小门卫 can't find it in its auth store.”。

原文小纸条

This means the run is pinned to an Anthropic auth profile, but the Gateway can't find it in its auth store.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Model failover and "All models failed"”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Use a setup-token**、Run claude setup-token, then paste it wit…、If the token was created on another machi…。

原文小纸条
  • **Use a setup-token**
  • Run claude setup-token, then paste it with openclaw models auth setup-token --provider anthropic.
  • If the token was created on another machine, use openclaw models auth paste-token --provider anthropic.
像准备清单

这一组条目像“Model failover and "All models failed"”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**If you want to use an 对话接口 key instead**、Put ANTHROPIC_对话接口_KEY in ~/.openclaw/.en…、Clear any pinned order that forces a miss…。

原文小纸条
  • **If you want to use an API key instead**
  • Put ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in ~/.openclaw/.env on the **gateway host**.
  • Clear any pinned order that forces a missing profile:
像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw models auth order clear --provider anthropic ``

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Model failover and "All models failed"”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：**Confirm you're running 魔法口令s on the 门口的…、In remote mode, auth profiles live on the…。

原文小纸条
  • **Confirm you're running commands on the gateway host**
  • In remote mode, auth profiles live on the gateway machine, not your laptop.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Why did it also try Google Gemini and fail?"> If your 聪明脑袋模型 设置说明书 includes Google Gemini as a fallba…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Why did it also try Google Gemini and fail?"> If your model config includes Google Gemini as a fallback (or you switched to a Gemini shorthand), OpenClaw will try it during model fallback. If you haven't configured Google credentials, you'll see No API key found for provider "google".

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Fix: either provide Google auth, or remove/avoid Google 聪明脑袋模型s in 机器人朋友s.defaults.聪明脑袋模型.fallbacks / aliases so fallba…”。

原文小纸条

Fix: either provide Google auth, or remove/avoid Google models in agents.defaults.model.fallbacks / aliases so fallback doesn't route there.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“**LLM request rejected: thinking signature required (Google Antigravity)**”。

**LLM request rejected: thinking signature required (Google Antigravity)**

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Cause: the session history contains **thinking blocks without signatures** (often from an aborted/partial 一边说一边送). Goog…”。

原文小纸条

Cause: the session history contains **thinking blocks without signatures** (often from an aborted/partial stream). Google Antigravity requires signatures for thinking blocks.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Fix: OpenClaw now strips unsigned thinking blocks for Google Antigravity Claude. If it still appears, start a **new ses…”。

原文小纸条

Fix: OpenClaw now strips unsigned thinking blocks for Google Antigravity Claude. If it still appears, start a **new session** or set /thinking off for that agent. </Accordion> </AccordionGroup>

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Auth profiles: what they are and how to manage them”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：anthropic:default (common when no email i…、anthropic:<email> for OAuth identities、custom IDs you choose (e.g. anthropic:wor…。

原文小纸条
  • anthropic:default (common when no email identity exists)
  • anthropic:<email> for OAuth identities
  • custom IDs you choose (e.g. anthropic:work)
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can I control which auth profile is tried first?"> Yes. 设置说明书 supports optional metadata for profiles…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I control which auth profile is tried first?"> Yes. Config supports optional metadata for profiles and an ordering per provider (auth.order.<provider>). This does **not** store secrets; it maps IDs to provider/mode and sets rotation order.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“OpenClaw may temporarily skip a profile if it's in a short **cooldown** (rate limits/timeouts/auth failures) or a longe…”。

原文小纸条

OpenClaw may temporarily skip a profile if it's in a short **cooldown** (rate limits/timeouts/auth failures) or a longer **disabled** state (billing/insufficient credits). To inspect this, run openclaw models status --json and check auth.unusableProfiles. Tuning: auth.cooldowns.billingBackoffHours*.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“You can also set a **per-机器人朋友** order override (stored in that 机器人朋友's auth-profiles.json) via the CLI:”。

原文小纸条

You can also set a **per-agent** order override (stored in that agent's auth-profiles.json) via the CLI:

原文小纸条

```bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} # Defaults to the configured default agent (omit --agent) openclaw models auth order get --provider anthropic

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Lock rotation to a single profile (only try this one) openclaw 聪明脑袋模型s auth order set --provider anthropic anthropic:de…”。

第 15 站

门口的小门卫: ports, "already running", and remote mode

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="What port does the 门口的小门卫 use?"> 门口的小门卫.port controls the single multiplexed port…”。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="What port does the Gateway use?"> gateway.port controls the single multiplexed port for WebSocket + HTTP (Control UI, hooks, etc.).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Precedence:”。

原文小纸条

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Why does openclaw 门口的小门卫 status say &#x22;Runtime: running&#x22; but &#x22;RPC probe: failed&#x22;?">…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Why does openclaw gateway status say &#x22;Runtime: running&#x22; but &#x22;RPC probe: failed&#x22;?"> Because "running" is the **supervisor's** view (launchd/systemd/schtasks). The RPC probe is the CLI actually connecting to the gateway WebSocket and calling status.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Gateway: ports, "already running", and remote mode”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Probe target: (the URL the probe actually…、Listening: (what's actually bound on the…、Last 门口的小门卫 error: (common root cause whe…。

原文小纸条
  • Probe target: (the URL the probe actually used)
  • Listening: (what's actually bound on the port)
  • Last gateway error: (common root cause when the process is alive but the port isn't listening)
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Why does openclaw 门口的小门卫 status show &#x22;设置说明书 (cli)&#x22; and &#x22;设置说明书 (service)&#x22; differen…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Why does openclaw gateway status show &#x22;Config (cli)&#x22; and &#x22;Config (service)&#x22; different?"> You're editing one config file while the service is running another (often a --profile / OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR mismatch).

像讲绘本

原文小纸条

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw gateway install --force ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Run that from the same --profile / environment you want the service to use. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Run that from the same --profile / environment you want the service to use. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="What does &#x22;another 门口的小门卫 instance is already listening&#x22; mean?"> OpenClaw enforces a runtim…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="What does &#x22;another gateway instance is already listening&#x22; mean?"> OpenClaw enforces a runtime lock by binding the WebSocket listener immediately on startup (default ws://127.0.0.1:18789). If the bind fails with EADDRINUSE, it throws GatewayLockError indicating another instance is already listening.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Fix: stop the other instance, free the port, or run with openclaw 门口的小门卫 --port <port>. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Fix: stop the other instance, free the port, or run with openclaw gateway --port <port>. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do I run OpenClaw in remote mode (来帮忙的小伙伴 connects to a 门口的小门卫 elsewhere)?"> Set 门口的小门卫.mode: "re…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I run OpenClaw in remote mode (client connects to a Gateway elsewhere)?"> Set gateway.mode: "remote" and point to a remote WebSocket URL, optionally with a token/password:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``json5 theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} { gateway: { mode: "remote", remote: { url: "ws://gateway.tailnet:18789", token: "your-token", password: "your-password", }, }, } ``

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Gateway: ports, "already running", and remote mode”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：openclaw 门口的小门卫 only starts when 门口的小门卫.m…、The macOS app watches the 设置说明书 file and…。

原文小纸条
  • openclaw gateway only starts when gateway.mode is local (or you pass the override flag).
  • The macOS app watches the config file and switches modes live when these values change.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="The Control UI says &#x22;unauthorized&#x22; (or keeps reconnecting). What now?"> Your 门口的小门卫 is runn…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="The Control UI says &#x22;unauthorized&#x22; (or keeps reconnecting). What now?"> Your gateway is running with auth enabled (gateway.auth.*), but the UI is not sending the matching token/password.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Facts (from code):”。

原文小纸条

Facts (from code):

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Gateway: ports, "already running", and remote mode”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：The Control UI keeps the token in session…、On AUTH_TOKEN_MISMATCH, trusted 来帮忙的小伙伴s…。

原文小纸条
  • The Control UI keeps the token in sessionStorage for the current browser tab session and selected gateway URL, so same-tab refreshes keep working without restoring long-lived localStorage token persistence.
  • On AUTH_TOKEN_MISMATCH, trusted clients can attempt one bounded retry with a cached device token when the gateway returns retry hints (canRetryWithDeviceToken=true, recommendedNextStep=retry_with_device_token).
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Fix:”。

原文小纸条

Fix:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“</Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

</Accordion>

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Gateway: ports, "already running", and remote mode”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Fastest: openclaw dashboard (prints + cop…、If you don't have a token yet: openclaw d…、If remote, tunnel first: ssh -N -L 18789:…、Set 门口的小门卫.auth.token (or OPENCLAW_门口的小门卫…。

原文小纸条
  • Fastest: openclaw dashboard (prints + copies the dashboard URL, tries to open; shows SSH hint if headless).
  • If you don't have a token yet: openclaw doctor --generate-gateway-token.
  • If remote, tunnel first: ssh -N -L 18789:127.0.0.1:18789 user@host then open http://127.0.0.1:18789/.
  • Set gateway.auth.token (or OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN) on the gateway host.
  • In the Control UI settings, paste the same token.
  • If mismatch persists after the one retry, rotate/re-approve the paired device token:
  • openclaw devices list
  • openclaw devices rotate --device <id> --role operator
  • Still stuck? Run openclaw status --all and follow Troubleshooting. See Dashboard for auth details.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="I set 门口的小门卫.bind tailnet but it cannot bind and nothing listens"> tailnet bind picks a Tailscale IP…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="I set gateway.bind tailnet but it cannot bind and nothing listens"> tailnet bind picks a Tailscale IP from your network interfaces (100.64.0.0/10). If the machine isn't on Tailscale (or the interface is down), there's nothing to bind to.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Fix:”。

原文小纸条

Fix:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Gateway: ports, "already running", and remote mode”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Start Tailscale on that host (so it has a…、Switch to 门口的小门卫.bind: "loopback" / "lan".

原文小纸条
  • Start Tailscale on that host (so it has a 100.x address), or
  • Switch to gateway.bind: "loopback" / "lan".
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Note: tailnet is explicit. auto prefers loopback; use 门口的小门卫.bind: "tailnet" when you want a tailnet-only bind. </Accor…”。

原文小纸条

Note: tailnet is explicit. auto prefers loopback; use gateway.bind: "tailnet" when you want a tailnet-only bind. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can I run multiple 门口的小门卫s on the same host?"> Usually no - one 门口的小门卫 can run multiple messaging 消息通…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I run multiple Gateways on the same host?"> Usually no - one Gateway can run multiple messaging channels and agents. Use multiple Gateways only when you need redundancy (ex: rescue bot) or hard isolation.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Yes, but you must isolate:”。

Yes, but you must isolate:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Gateway: ports, "already running", and remote mode”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：OPENCLAW_设置说明书_PATH (per-instance 设置说明书)、OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR (per-instance state)、机器人朋友s.defaults.workspace (workspace isol…、门口的小门卫.port (unique ports)。

原文小纸条
  • OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH (per-instance config)
  • OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR (per-instance state)
  • agents.defaults.workspace (workspace isolation)
  • gateway.port (unique ports)
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Quick setup (recommended):”。

原文小纸条

Quick setup (recommended):

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Gateway: ports, "already running", and remote mode”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Use openclaw --profile <name> ... per ins…、Set a unique 门口的小门卫.port in each profile…、Install a per-profile service: openclaw -…。

原文小纸条
  • Use openclaw --profile <name> ... per instance (auto-creates ~/.openclaw-<name>).
  • Set a unique gateway.port in each profile config (or pass --port for manual runs).
  • Install a per-profile service: openclaw --profile <name> gateway install.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Profiles also suffix service names (ai.openclaw.<profile>; legacy com.openclaw.*, openclaw-门口的小门卫-<profile>.service, Op…”。

原文小纸条

Profiles also suffix service names (ai.openclaw.<profile>; legacy com.openclaw.*, openclaw-gateway-<profile>.service, OpenClaw Gateway (<profile>)). Full guide: Multiple gateways. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="What does &#x22;invalid handshake&#x22; / code 1008 mean?"> The 门口的小门卫 is a **WebSocket 大房子服务器**, and…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="What does &#x22;invalid handshake&#x22; / code 1008 mean?"> The Gateway is a **WebSocket server**, and it expects the very first message to be a connect frame. If it receives anything else, it closes the connection with **code 1008** (policy violation).

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Common causes:”。

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Gateway: ports, "already running", and remote mode”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：You opened the **HTTP** URL in a browser…、You used the wrong port or path.、A proxy or tunnel stripped auth headers o…。

原文小纸条
  • You opened the **HTTP** URL in a browser (http://...) instead of a WS client.
  • You used the wrong port or path.
  • A proxy or tunnel stripped auth headers or sent a non-Gateway request.

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Quick fixes:”。

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“1. Use the WS URL: ws://<host>:18789 (or wss://... if HTTPS). 2. Don't open the WS port in a normal browser tab. 3. If…”。

原文小纸条

1. Use the WS URL: ws://<host>:18789 (or wss://... if HTTPS). 2. Don't open the WS port in a normal browser tab. 3. If auth is on, include the token/password in the connect frame.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Logging and debugging”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：macOS: $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/logs/门口的小门卫.lo…、Linux: journalctl --user -u openclaw-门口的小…、Windows: schtasks /Query /TN "OpenClaw 门口…。

原文小纸条
  • macOS: $OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/logs/gateway.log and gateway.err.log (default: ~/.openclaw/logs/...; profiles use ~/.openclaw-<profile>/logs/...)
  • Linux: journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway[-<profile>].service -n 200 --no-pager
  • Windows: schtasks /Query /TN "OpenClaw Gateway (<profile>)" /V /FO LIST

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Logging and debugging”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：聪明脑袋模型 auth not loaded on the **门口的小门卫 ho…、消息通道 pairing/allowlist blocking replies (…、WebChat/Dashboard is open without the rig…。

原文小纸条
  • Model auth not loaded on the **gateway host** (check models status).
  • Channel pairing/allowlist blocking replies (check channel config + logs).
  • WebChat/Dashboard is open without the right token.

原文小纸条

Then match the error:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Logging and debugging”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：BOT_魔法口令S_TOO_MUCH: the Telegram menu has…、TypeError: fetch failed, Network request…。

原文小纸条
  • BOT_COMMANDS_TOO_MUCH: the Telegram menu has too many entries. OpenClaw already trims to the Telegram limit and retries with fewer commands, but some menu entries still need to be dropped. Reduce plugin/skill/custom commands, or disable channels.telegram.commands.native if you do not need the menu.
  • TypeError: fetch failed, Network request for 'setMyCommands' failed!, or similar network errors: if you are on a VPS or behind a proxy, confirm outbound HTTPS is allowed and DNS works for api.telegram.org.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If the 门口的小门卫 is remote, make sure you are looking at logs on the 门口的小门卫 host.”。

原文小纸条

If the Gateway is remote, make sure you are looking at logs on the Gateway host.

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Logging and debugging”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：openclaw 门口的小门卫 restart: restarts the **b…、openclaw 门口的小门卫: runs the 门口的小门卫 **in the…。

原文小纸条
  • openclaw gateway restart: restarts the **background service** (launchd/systemd).
  • openclaw gateway: runs the gateway **in the foreground** for this terminal session.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If you installed the service, use the 门口的小门卫 魔法口令s. Use openclaw 门口的小门卫 when you want a one-off, foreground run. </Acco…”。

原文小纸条

If you installed the service, use the gateway commands. Use openclaw gateway when you want a one-off, foreground run. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Fastest way to get more details when something fails"> Start the 门口的小门卫 with --verbose to get more co…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Fastest way to get more details when something fails"> Start the Gateway with --verbose to get more console detail. Then inspect the log file for channel auth, model routing, and RPC errors. </Accordion> </AccordionGroup>

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="My skill generated an image/PDF, but nothing was sent"> Outbound attachments from…”。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="My skill generated an image/PDF, but nothing was sent"> Outbound attachments from the agent must include a MEDIA:<path-or-url> line (on its own line). See OpenClaw assistant setup and Agent send.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“CLI sending:”。

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Media and attachments”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：The target 消息通道 supports outbound media a…、The file is within the provider's size li…。

原文小纸条
  • The target channel supports outbound media and isn't blocked by allowlists.
  • The file is within the provider's size limits (images are resized to max 2048px).

第 18 站

安全守门员 and access control

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“安全守门员 and access control”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="Is it safe to expose OpenClaw to inbound DMs?"> Treat inbound DMs as untrusted inp…”。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="Is it safe to expose OpenClaw to inbound DMs?"> Treat inbound DMs as untrusted input. Defaults are designed to reduce risk:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Security and access control”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Default behavior on DM-capable 消息通道s is *…、Unknown senders receive a pairing code; t…、Approve with: openclaw pairing approve --…、Pending requests are capped at **3 per 消息…。

原文小纸条
  • Default behavior on DM-capable channels is **pairing**:
  • Unknown senders receive a pairing code; the bot does not process their message.
  • Approve with: openclaw pairing approve --channel <channel> [--account <id>] <code>
  • Pending requests are capped at **3 per channel**; check openclaw pairing list --channel <channel> [--account <id>] if a code didn't arrive.
  • Opening DMs publicly requires explicit opt-in (dmPolicy: "open" and allowlist "*").
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Run openclaw doctor to surface risky DM policies. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Run openclaw doctor to surface risky DM policies. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Is prompt injection only a concern for public bots?"> No. Prompt injection is about **untrusted conte…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Is prompt injection only a concern for public bots?"> No. Prompt injection is about **untrusted content**, not just who can DM the bot. If your assistant reads external content (web search/fetch, browser pages, emails, docs, attachments, pasted logs), that content can include instructions that try to hijack the model. This can happen even if **you are the only sender**.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“The biggest risk is when 工具小帮手s are enabled: the 聪明脑袋模型 can be tricked into exfiltrating context or calling 工具小帮手s on y…”。

原文小纸条

The biggest risk is when tools are enabled: the model can be tricked into exfiltrating context or calling tools on your behalf. Reduce the blast radius by:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Security and access control”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：using a read-only or 工具小帮手-disabled "read…、keeping web_search / web_fetch / browser…、sandboxing and strict 工具小帮手 allowlists。

原文小纸条
  • using a read-only or tool-disabled "reader" agent to summarize untrusted content
  • keeping web_search / web_fetch / browser off for tool-enabled agents
  • sandboxing and strict tool allowlists
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Details: 安全守门员. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Details: Security. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Should my bot have its own email, GitHub account, or phone number?"> Yes, for most setups. Isolating…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Should my bot have its own email, GitHub account, or phone number?"> Yes, for most setups. Isolating the bot with separate accounts and phone numbers reduces the blast radius if something goes wrong. This also makes it easier to rotate credentials or revoke access without impacting your personal accounts.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Start small. Give access only to the 工具小帮手s and accounts you actually need, and expand later if required.”。

原文小纸条

Start small. Give access only to the tools and accounts you actually need, and expand later if required.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: 安全守门员, Pairing. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

Docs: Security, Pairing. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can I give it autonomy over my text 小纸条消息s and is that safe?"> We do **not** recommend full autonomy…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I give it autonomy over my text messages and is that safe?"> We do **not** recommend full autonomy over your personal messages. The safest pattern is:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Security and access control”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：Keep DMs in **pairing mode** or a tight a…、Use a **separate number or account** if y…、Let it draft, then **approve before sendi…。

原文小纸条
  • Keep DMs in **pairing mode** or a tight allowlist.
  • Use a **separate number or account** if you want it to message on your behalf.
  • Let it draft, then **approve before sending**.
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If you want to experiment, do it on a dedicated account and keep it isolated. See 安全守门员. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

If you want to experiment, do it on a dedicated account and keep it isolated. See Security. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Can I use cheaper 聪明脑袋模型s for personal assistant tasks?"> Yes, **if** the 机器人朋友 is chat-only and the…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Can I use cheaper models for personal assistant tasks?"> Yes, **if** the agent is chat-only and the input is trusted. Smaller tiers are more susceptible to instruction hijacking, so avoid them for tool-enabled agents or when reading untrusted content. If you must use a smaller model, lock down tools and run inside a sandbox. See Security. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="I ran /start in Telegram but did not get a pairing code"> Pairing codes are sent **only** when an unk…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="I ran /start in Telegram but did not get a pairing code"> Pairing codes are sent **only** when an unknown sender messages the bot and dmPolicy: "pairing" is enabled. /start by itself doesn't generate a code.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Check pending requests:”。

原文小纸条

Check pending requests:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``bash theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} openclaw pairing list telegram ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If you want immediate access, allowlist your sender id or set dmPolicy: "open" for that account. </Accordion>”。

原文小纸条

If you want immediate access, allowlist your sender id or set dmPolicy: "open" for that account. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="WhatsApp: will it 小纸条消息 my contacts? How does pairing work?"> No. Default WhatsApp DM policy is **pai…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="WhatsApp: will it message my contacts? How does pairing work?"> No. Default WhatsApp DM policy is **pairing**. Unknown senders only get a pairing code and their message is **not processed**. OpenClaw only replies to chats it receives or to explicit sends you trigger.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Approve pairing with:”。

原文小纸条

Approve pairing with:

原文小纸条

List pending requests:

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Wizard phone number prompt: it's used to set your **allowlist/owner** so your own DMs are permitted. It's not used for…”。

原文小纸条

Wizard phone number prompt: it's used to set your **allowlist/owner** so your own DMs are permitted. It's not used for auto-sending. If you run on your personal WhatsApp number, use that number and enable channels.whatsapp.selfChatMode. </Accordion> </AccordionGroup>

第 19 站

Chat 魔法口令s, aborting tasks, and "it will not stop"

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="How do I stop internal system 小纸条消息s from showing in chat?"> Most internal or 工具小帮…”。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="How do I stop internal system messages from showing in chat?"> Most internal or tool messages only appear when **verbose** or **reasoning** is enabled for that session.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Fix in the chat where you see it:”。

Fix in the chat where you see it:

原文小纸条

`` /verbose off /reasoning off ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“If it is still noisy, check the session settings in the Control UI and set verbose to **inherit**. Also confirm you are…”。

原文小纸条

If it is still noisy, check the session settings in the Control UI and set verbose to **inherit**. Also confirm you are not using a bot profile with verboseDefault set to on in config.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Docs: Thinking and verbose, 安全守门员. </Accordion>”。

Docs: Thinking and verbose, Security. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do I stop/cancel a running task?"> Send any of these **as a standalone 小纸条消息** (no slash):”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I stop/cancel a running task?"> Send any of these **as a standalone message** (no slash):

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

`` stop stop action stop current action stop run stop current run stop agent stop the agent stop openclaw openclaw stop stop don't do anything stop do not do anything stop doing anything please stop stop please abort esc wait exit interrupt ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“These are abort triggers (not slash 魔法口令s).”。

These are abort triggers (not slash commands).

原文小纸条

For background processes (from the exec tool), you can ask the agent to run:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

`` process action:kill sessionId:XXX ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Slash 魔法口令s overview: see Slash 魔法口令s.”。

原文小纸条

Slash commands overview: see Slash commands.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Most 魔法口令s must be sent as a **standalone** 小纸条消息 that starts with /, but a few shortcuts (like /status) also work inli…”。

原文小纸条

Most commands must be sent as a **standalone** message that starts with /, but a few shortcuts (like /status) also work inline for allowlisted senders. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="How do I send a Discord 小纸条消息 from Telegram? (&#x22;Cross-context messaging denied&#x22;)"> OpenClaw…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="How do I send a Discord message from Telegram? (&#x22;Cross-context messaging denied&#x22;)"> OpenClaw blocks **cross-provider** messaging by default. If a tool call is bound to Telegram, it won't send to Discord unless you explicitly allow it.

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Enable cross-provider messaging for the 机器人朋友:”。

原文小纸条

Enable cross-provider messaging for the agent:

像讲绘本

这一小段像旁白，提醒我们现在讲到哪一步了。

原文小纸条

``json5 theme={"theme":{"light":"min-light","dark":"min-dark"}} { agents: { defaults: { tools: { message: { crossContext: { allowAcrossProviders: true, marker: { enabled: true, prefix: "[from {channel}] " }, }, }, }, }, }, } ``

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Restart the 门口的小门卫 after editing 设置说明书. If you only want this for a single 机器人朋友, set it under 机器人朋友s.list[].工具小帮手s.小纸条…”。

原文小纸条

Restart the gateway after editing config. If you only want this for a single agent, set it under agents.list[].tools.message instead. </Accordion>

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion title="Why does it feel like the bot &#x22;ignores&#x22; r对话接口d-fire 小纸条消息s?"> Queue mode controls how new 小…”。

原文小纸条

<Accordion title="Why does it feel like the bot &#x22;ignores&#x22; rapid-fire messages?"> Queue mode controls how new messages interact with an in-flight run. Use /queue to change modes:

像准备清单

这一组条目像“Chat commands, aborting tasks, and "it will not stop"”的小卡片，告诉我们要准备哪些东西、哪些规则不能漏掉。先记住这几个重点：steer - new 小纸条消息s redirect the current t…、followup - run 小纸条消息s one at a time、collect - batch 小纸条消息s and reply once (de…、steer-backlog - steer now, then process b…。

原文小纸条
  • steer - new messages redirect the current task
  • followup - run messages one at a time
  • collect - batch messages and reply once (default)
  • steer-backlog - steer now, then process backlog
  • interrupt - abort current run and start fresh
像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“You can add options like debounce:2s cap:25 drop:summarize for followup modes. </Accordion> </Accordion大家一起的房间>”。

原文小纸条

You can add options like debounce:2s cap:25 drop:summarize for followup modes. </Accordion> </AccordionGroup>

第 20 站

Miscellaneous

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“Miscellaneous”。

像讲绘本

把它讲给 5 岁小朋友听，就是：先认识这里出现的几个角色和规则，再按顺序照着做。这里最重要的意思是“<Accordion大家一起的房间> <Accordion title="What is the default 聪明脑袋模型 for Anthropic with an 对话接口 key?"> In OpenClaw, credenti…”。

原文小纸条

<AccordionGroup> <Accordion title="What is the default model for Anthropic with an API key?"> In OpenClaw, credentials and model selection are separate. Setting ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (or storing an Anthropic API key in auth profiles) enables authentication, but the actual default model is whatever you configure in agents.defaults.model.primary (for example, anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 or anthropic/claude-opus-4-6). If you see No credentials found for profile "anthropic:default", it means the Gateway couldn't find Anthropic credentials in the expected auth-profiles.json for the agent that's running. </Accordion> </AccordionGroup>

像讲绘本
